L. 114–10, title I, § 106(b)(1)129 Stat. 138Apr. 16, 2015“(A).—ObjectiveAs a consequence of a significant Federal investment in the implementation of health information technology through the Medicare and Medicaid EHR incentive programs, Congress declares it a national objective to achieve widespread exchange of health information through interoperable certified EHR technology nationwide by .December 31, 2018“(B).—DefinitionsIn this paragraph:“(i).—Widespread interoperabilityThe term ‘widespread interoperability’ means interoperability between certified EHR technology systems employed by meaningful EHR users under the Medicare and Medicaid EHR incentive programs and other clinicians and health care providers on a nationwide basis.“(ii).—InteroperabilityThe term ‘interoperability’ means the ability of two or more health information systems or components to exchange clinical and other information and to use the information that has been exchanged using common standards as to provide access to longitudinal information for health care providers in order to facilitate coordinated care and improved patient outcomes.“(C).—Establishment of metricsNot later than , and in consultation with stakeholders, the Secretary [of Health and Human Services] shall establish metrics to be used to determine if and to the extent that the objective described in subparagraph (A) has been achieved.July 1, 2016“(D).—Recommendations if objective not achievedIf the Secretary of Health and Human Services determines that the objective described in subparagraph (A) has not been achieved by , then the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report, by not later than , that identifies barriers to such objective and recommends actions that the Federal Government can take to achieve such objective. B, § 1002127 Stat. 1195Dec. 26, 2013“In order to support the provision of quality care for our nation’s seniors, Congress finds it appropriate to reform physician reimbursements under the Medicare program. SGR reform legislation provides such an opportunity, but not until next year. In order to facilitate such reform, Congress finds that the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services should continue to focus its efforts on the following areas:“(1).—Simplify and reduce administrative burden on physiciansThe application and assessment of measures and other activities under SGR reform should be facilitated by the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) in a way that accounts for the administrative burden such measurement places on physicians. Therefore, the Congress encourages CMS to identify and implement, to the extent practicable, mechanisms to ensure that the application and assessment of measures be coordinated across programs.“(2).—Timely feedback for physiciansIn order for measure and assessment programs to encourage the highest quality care for Medicare seniors, the Congress finds it critical that CMS provide physicians with feedback on performance in as close to real time as possible. Such timely feedback will ensure that physicians can excel under a system of meaningful measurement.“(3).—Encourage development of new modelsThere is great need to test alternatives to Fee-For-Service reimbursement in the Medicare program. One option is the promotion and adoption of new models of care for physicians. To date, there has been significant development and testing of models for primary care. Congress supports these efforts and encourages them to continue in the future. Congress also encourages the development and testing of models of specialty care.”

Study of Regional Variations in Impact of Medicare Physician Payment Reform, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–508, title IV, § 4115104 Stat. 1388–65Nov. 5, 1990“(a).—StudyThe Secretary of Health and Human Services shall conduct a study of—“(1)factors that may explain geographic variations in Medicare reasonable charges for physicians’ services that are not attributable to variations in physician practice costs (including the supply of physicians in an area and area variations in the mix of services furnished);“(2)the extent to which the geographic practice cost indices applied under the fee schedule established under section 1848 of the Social Security Act [] accurately reflect variations in practice costs and malpractice costs (and alternative sources of information upon which to base such indices);42 U.S.C. 1395w–4“(3)the impact of the transition to a national, resource-based fee schedule for physicians’ services under Medicare on access to physicians’ services in areas that experience a disproportionately large reduction in payments for physicians’ services under the fee schedule by reason of such variations; and“(4)appropriate adjustments or modifications in the transition to, or manner of determining payments under, the fee schedule established under section 1848 of the Social Security Act, to compensate for such variations and ensure continued access to physicians’ services for Medicare beneficiaries in such areas.“(b).—ReportBy not later than , the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the study conducted under subsection (a).”July 1, 1992

Studies, , , as amended by , , ; , , , provided for various studies and reports as follows: (1) directed Comptroller General to conduct study of alternative payment methodology for malpractice component for physicians’ services, and to submit report to Congress by not later than ; (2) directed Secretary of Health and Human Services to conduct study of how payments under this section may affect payments to eligible organizations with risk-sharing contracts under , and to submit report to Congress by not later than ; (3) directed Secretary to conduct study of volume performance standard rates of increase for services furnished by geography, specialty, and type of service, and to submit report with appropriate recommendations to Congress by not later than ; (4) directed Physician Payment Review Commission to conduct study of payment for practice and malpractice expenses, including appropriate methods for allocating malpractice expenses to particular procedures which could be incorporated into the determination of relative values for such procedures using a consensus panel and other appropriate methodologies, and to submit report and recommendations to Congress by not later than ; (5) directed Physician Payment Review Commission to conduct study of feasibility and desirability of using Metropolitan Statistical Areas or other payment areas for purposes of payment for physicians’ services under this part, and to submit report to Congress by not later than ; (6) directed Physician Payment Review Commission to conduct study of payment for non-physician providers of medicare services, including physician assistants, clinical psychologists, nurse midwives, and other health practitioners whose services can be billed under medicare program on a fee-for-service basis, and to submit report to Congress by not later than ; (7) directed Physician Payment Review Commission to conduct study of physician fees under State medicaid programs established under subchapter XIX of this chapter, and to submit report with recommendations to Congress by no later than ; and (8) directed Comptroller General to conduct study of effect of anti-trust laws on ability of physicians to act in groups to educate and discipline peers of such physicians in order to reduce and eliminate ineffective practice patterns and inappropriate utilization, and to submit report to Congress by no later than .Pub. Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services To Study Reform of Physician Reimbursements, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–67, div. B, § 1002127 Stat. 1195Dec. 26, 2013“In order to support the provision of quality care for our nation’s seniors, Congress finds it appropriate to reform physician reimbursements under the Medicare program. SGR reform legislation provides such an opportunity, but not until next year. In order to facilitate such reform, Congress finds that the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services should continue to focus its efforts on the following areas:“(1).—Simplify and reduce administrative burden on physiciansThe application and assessment of measures and other activities under SGR reform should be facilitated by the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) in a way that accounts for the administrative burden such measurement places on physicians. Therefore, the Congress encourages CMS to identify and implement, to the extent practicable, mechanisms to ensure that the application and assessment of measures be coordinated across programs.“(2).—Timely feedback for physiciansIn order for measure and assessment programs to encourage the highest quality care for Medicare seniors, the Congress finds it critical that CMS provide physicians with feedback on performance in as close to real time as possible. Such timely feedback will ensure that physicians can excel under a system of meaningful measurement.“(3).—Encourage development of new modelsThere is great need to test alternatives to Fee-For-Service reimbursement in the Medicare program. One option is the promotion and adoption of new models of care for physicians. To date, there has been significant development and testing of models for primary care. Congress supports these efforts and encourages them to continue in the future. Congress also encourages the development and testing of models of specialty care.”

L. 112–96, title III, § 3006126 Stat. 189Dec. 21, 2000Dec. 8, 2003Dec. 20, 2006Dec. 29, 2007July 15, 2008Mar. 23, 2010Dec. 15, 2010Dec. 23, 2011Feb. 22, 2012“(a).—In GeneralWhen an independent laboratory furnishes the technical component of a physician pathology service to a fee-for-service medicare beneficiary who is an inpatient or outpatient of a covered hospital, the Secretary of Health and Human Services shall treat such component as a service for which payment shall be made to the laboratory under section 1848 of the Social Security Act () and not as an inpatient hospital service for which payment is made to the hospital under section 1886(d) of such Act () or as an outpatient hospital service for which payment is made to the hospital under section 1833(t) of such Act ((t)).42 U.S.C. 1395w–442 U.S.C. 1395ww(d)42 U.S.C. 1395l“(b).—DefinitionsFor purposes of this section:“(1).—Covered hospitalThe term ‘covered hospital’ means, with respect to an inpatient or an outpatient, a hospital that had an arrangement with an independent laboratory that was in effect as of , under which a laboratory furnished the technical component of physician pathology services to fee-for-service medicare beneficiaries who were hospital inpatients or outpatients, respectively, and submitted claims for payment for such component to a medicare carrier (that has a contract with the Secretary under section 1842 of the Social Security Act, ) and not to such hospital.July 22, 199942 U.S.C. 1395u“(2).—Fee-for-service medicare beneficiaryThe term ‘fee-for-service medicare beneficiary’ means an individual who—“(A)is entitled to benefits under part A, or enrolled under part B, or both, of such title [ et seq., 1395j et seq.]; and42 U.S.C. 1395c“(B)is not enrolled in any of the following:“(i)A Medicare+Choice plan under part C of such title [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1395w–21“(ii)A plan offered by an eligible organization under section 1876 of such Act ().42 U.S.C. 1395mm“(iii)A program of all-inclusive care for the elderly (PACE) under section 1894 of such Act ().42 U.S.C. 1395eee“(iv)A social health maintenance organization (SHMO) demonstration project established under section 4018(b) of the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1987 () [].Public Law 100–203101 Stat. 1330–65“(c).—Effective DateThis section shall apply to services furnished during the 2-year period beginning on , and for services furnished during 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, and the first six months of 2012.January 1, 2001“(d)GAO Report.—“(1).—StudyThe Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct a study of the effects of the previous provisions of this section on hospitals and laboratories and access of fee-for-service medicare beneficiaries to the technical component of physician pathology services.“(2).—ReportNot later than , the Comptroller General shall submit to Congress a report on such study. The report shall include recommendations about whether such provisions should be extended after the end of the period specified in subsection (c) for either or both inpatient and outpatient hospital services, and whether the provisions should be extended to other hospitals.”April 1, 2002

Studies, , , as amended by , , ; , , , provided for various studies and reports as follows: (1) directed Comptroller General to conduct study of alternative payment methodology for malpractice component for physicians’ services, and to submit report to Congress by not later than ; (2) directed Secretary of Health and Human Services to conduct study of how payments under this section may affect payments to eligible organizations with risk-sharing contracts under , and to submit report to Congress by not later than ; (3) directed Secretary to conduct study of volume performance standard rates of increase for services furnished by geography, specialty, and type of service, and to submit report with appropriate recommendations to Congress by not later than ; (4) directed Physician Payment Review Commission to conduct study of payment for practice and malpractice expenses, including appropriate methods for allocating malpractice expenses to particular procedures which could be incorporated into the determination of relative values for such procedures using a consensus panel and other appropriate methodologies, and to submit report and recommendations to Congress by not later than ; (5) directed Physician Payment Review Commission to conduct study of feasibility and desirability of using Metropolitan Statistical Areas or other payment areas for purposes of payment for physicians’ services under this part, and to submit report to Congress by not later than ; (6) directed Physician Payment Review Commission to conduct study of payment for non-physician providers of medicare services, including physician assistants, clinical psychologists, nurse midwives, and other health practitioners whose services can be billed under medicare program on a fee-for-service basis, and to submit report to Congress by not later than ; (7) directed Physician Payment Review Commission to conduct study of physician fees under State medicaid programs established under subchapter XIX of this chapter, and to submit report with recommendations to Congress by no later than ; and (8) directed Comptroller General to conduct study of effect of anti-trust laws on ability of physicians to act in groups to educate and discipline peers of such physicians in order to reduce and eliminate ineffective practice patterns and inappropriate utilization, and to submit report to Congress by no later than .Pub. children.heading: Payment based on fee schedule
children.label: a Payment based on fee schedule
A
children.text: the actual charge for the service, or

children.label: B
children.text: subject to the succeeding provisions of this subsection, the amount determined under the fee schedule established under subsection (b) for services furnished during that year (in this subsection referred to as the “fee schedule amount”).

children.heading: Limiting reductions and increases to 15 percent in 1992
children.label: A Limiting reductions and increases to 15 percent in 1992

children.heading: Limit on increase
children.label: i Limit on increase
children.text: In the case of a service in a fee schedule area (as defined in subsection (j)(2)) for which the adjusted historical payment basis (as defined in subparagraph (D)) is less than 85 percent of the fee schedule amount for services furnished in 1992, there shall be substituted for the fee schedule amount an amount equal to the adjusted historical payment basis plus 15 percent of the fee schedule amount otherwise established (without regard to this paragraph).

children.heading: Limit in reduction
children.label: ii Limit in reduction
children.text: In the case of a service in a fee schedule area for which the adjusted historical payment basis exceeds 115 percent of the fee schedule amount for services furnished in 1992, there shall be substituted for the fee schedule amount an amount equal to the adjusted historical payment basis minus 15 percent of the fee schedule amount otherwise established (without regard to this paragraph).

children.label: I
children.text: 75 percent of the fee schedule amount determined under subparagraph (A), adjusted by the update established under subsection (d)(3) for 1993, and

children.label: II
children.text: 25 percent of the fee schedule amount determined under paragraph (1) for 1993 without regard to this paragraph;

children.label: I
children.text: 67 percent of the fee schedule amount determined under clause (i), adjusted by the update established under subsection (d)(3) for 1994 and as adjusted under subsection (c)(2)(F)(ii) and under section 13515(b) of the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1993, and

children.label: II
children.text: 33 percent of the fee schedule amount determined under paragraph (1) for 1994 without regard to this paragraph; and

children.label: I
children.text: 50 percent of the fee schedule amount determined under clause (ii) adjusted by the update established under subsection (d)(3) for 1995, and

children.label: II
children.text: 50 percent of the fee schedule amount determined under paragraph (1) for 1995 without regard to this paragraph.

children.heading: Special rule for anesthesia and radiology services
children.label: C Special rule for anesthesia and radiology services
children.text: With respect to physicians’ services which are anesthesia services, the Secretary shall provide for a transition in the same manner as a transition is provided for other services under subparagraph (B). With respect to radiology services, “109 percent” and “9 percent” shall be substituted for “115 percent” and “15 percent”, respectively, in subparagraph (A)(ii).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “adjusted historical payment basis” means, with respect to a physicians’ service furnished in a fee schedule area, the weighted average prevailing charge applied in the area for the service in 1991 (as determined by the Secretary without regard to physician specialty and as adjusted to reflect payments for services with customary charges below the prevailing charge or other payment limitations imposed by law or regulation) adjusted by the update established under subsection (d)(3) for 1992.

children.heading: Application to radiology services
children.label: ii Application to radiology services
children.text: In applying clause (i) in the case of physicians’ services which are radiology services (including radiologist services, as defined in ), but excluding nuclear medicine services that are subject to section 6105(b) of the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1989, there shall be substituted for the weighted average prevailing charge the amount provided under the fee schedule established for the service for the fee schedule area under .section 1395m(b)(6) of this titlesection 1395m(b) of this title

children.heading: Nuclear medicine services
children.label: iii Nuclear medicine services
children.text: In applying clause (i) in the case of physicians’ services which are nuclear medicine services, there shall be substituted for the weighted average prevailing charge the amount provided under section 6105(b) of the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1989.

children.heading: Incentives for participating physicians and suppliers
children.label: 3 Incentives for participating physicians and suppliers
children.text: In applying paragraph (1)(B) in the case of a nonparticipating physician or a nonparticipating supplier or other person, the fee schedule amount shall be 95 percent of such amount otherwise applied under this subsection (without regard to this paragraph). In the case of physicians’ services (including services which the Secretary excludes pursuant to subsection (j)(3)) of a nonparticipating physician, supplier, or other person for which payment is made under this part on a basis other than the fee schedule amount, the payment shall be based on 95 percent of the payment basis for such services furnished by a participating physician, supplier, or other person.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: With respect to physicians’ services furnished on or after , and consisting of medical direction of two, three, or four concurrent anesthesia cases, except as provided in paragraph (5), the fee schedule amount to be applied shall be equal to one-half of the amount described in subparagraph (B).January 1, 1994

children.label: i
children.text: For services furnished during 1994, 120 percent.

children.label: ii
children.text: For services furnished during 1995, 115 percent.

children.label: iii
children.text: For services furnished during 1996, 110 percent.

children.label: iv
children.text: For services furnished during 1997, 105 percent.

children.label: v
children.text: For services furnished after 1997, 100 percent.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B) and subsection (m)(2)(B), with respect to covered professional services furnished by an eligible professional during 2012, 2013 or 2014, if the eligible professional is not a successful electronic prescriber for the reporting period for the year (as determined under subsection (m)(3)(B)), the fee schedule amount for such services furnished by such professional during the year (including the fee schedule amount for purposes of determining a payment based on such amount) shall be equal to the applicable percent of the fee schedule amount that would otherwise apply to such services under this subsection (determined after application of paragraph (3) but without regard to this paragraph).

children.label: I
children.text: for 2012, 99 percent;

children.label: II
children.text: for 2013, 98.5 percent; and

children.label: III
children.text: for 2014, 98 percent.

children.heading: Significant hardship exception
children.label: B Significant hardship exception
children.text: The Secretary may, on a case-by-case basis, exempt an eligible professional from the application of the payment adjustment under subparagraph (A) if the Secretary determines, subject to annual renewal, that compliance with the requirement for being a successful electronic prescriber would result in a significant hardship, such as in the case of an eligible professional who practices in a rural area without sufficient Internet access.

children.heading: Physician reporting system rules
children.label: i Physician reporting system rules
children.text: Paragraphs (5), (6), and (8) of subsection (k) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph in the same manner as they apply for purposes of such subsection.

children.heading: Incentive payment validation rules
children.label: ii Incentive payment validation rules
children.text: Clauses (ii) and (iii) of subsection (m)(5)(D) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph in a similar manner as they apply for purposes of such subsection.

children.heading: Eligible professional; covered professional services
children.label: i Eligible professional; covered professional services
children.text: The terms “eligible professional” and “covered professional services” have the meanings given such terms in subsection (k)(3).

children.heading: Physician reporting system
children.label: ii Physician reporting system
children.text: The term “physician reporting system” means the system established under subsection (k).

children.heading: Reporting period
children.label: iii Reporting period
children.text: The term “reporting period” means, with respect to a year, a period specified by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: the teaching anesthesiologist is present during all critical or key portions of the anesthesia service or procedure involved; and

children.label: B
children.text: the teaching anesthesiologist (or another anesthesiologist with whom the teaching anesthesiologist has entered into an arrangement) is immediately available to furnish anesthesia services during the entire procedure.

children.heading: Incentives for meaningful use of certified EHR technology
children.label: 7 Incentives for meaningful use of certified EHR technology

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraphs (B) and (D), with respect to covered professional services furnished by an eligible professional during each of 2015 through 2018, if the eligible professional is not a meaningful EHR user (as determined under subsection ()(2)) for an EHR reporting period for the year, the fee schedule amount for such services furnished by such professional during the year (including the fee schedule amount for purposes of determining a payment based on such amount) shall be equal to the applicable percent of the fee schedule amount that would otherwise apply to such services under this subsection (determined after application of paragraph (3) but without regard to this paragraph).o

children.label: I
children.text: for 2015, 99 percent (or, in the case of an eligible professional who was subject to the application of the payment adjustment under subsection (a)(5) for 2014, 98 percent);

children.label: II
children.text: for 2016, 98 percent; and

children.label: III
children.text: for 2017 and 2018, 97 percent.

children.heading: Authority to decrease applicable percentage for 2018
children.label: iii Authority to decrease applicable percentage for 2018
children.text: For 2018, if the Secretary finds that the proportion of eligible professionals who are meaningful EHR users (as determined under subsection ()(2)) is less than 75 percent, the applicable percent shall be decreased by 1 percentage point from the applicable percent in the preceding year.o

children.heading: Significant hardship exception
children.label: B Significant hardship exception
children.text: The Secretary may, on a case-by-case basis (and, with respect to the payment adjustment under subparagraph (A) for 2017, for categories of eligible professionals, as established by the Secretary and posted on the Internet website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services prior to , an application for which must be submitted to the Secretary by not later than ), exempt an eligible professional from the application of the payment adjustment under subparagraph (A) if the Secretary determines, subject to annual renewal, that compliance with the requirement for being a meaningful EHR user would result in a significant hardship, such as in the case of an eligible professional who practices in a rural area without sufficient Internet access. The Secretary shall exempt an eligible professional from the application of the payment adjustment under subparagraph (A) with respect to a year, subject to annual renewal, if the Secretary determines that compliance with the requirement for being a meaningful EHR user is not possible because the certified EHR technology used by such professional has been decertified under a program kept or recognized pursuant to . In no case may an eligible professional be granted an exemption under this subparagraph for more than 5 years.December 15, 2015March 15, 2016section 300jj–11(c)(5) of this title

children.heading: Application of physician reporting system rules
children.label: C Application of physician reporting system rules
children.text: Paragraphs (5), (6), and (8) of subsection (k) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph in the same manner as they apply for purposes of such subsection.

children.heading: Non-application to hospital-based and ambulatory surgical center-based eligible professionals
children.label: D Non-application to hospital-based and ambulatory surgical center-based eligible professionals

children.heading: Hospital-based
children.label: i Hospital-based
children.text: No payment adjustment may be made under subparagraph (A) in the case of hospital-based eligible professionals (as defined in subsection ()(1)(C)(ii)).o

children.heading: Ambulatory surgical center-based
children.label: ii Ambulatory surgical center-based
children.text: Subject to clause (iv), no payment adjustment may be made under subparagraph (A) for 2017 and 2018 in the case of an eligible professional with respect to whom substantially all of the covered professional services furnished by such professional are furnished in an ambulatory surgical center.

children.label: I
children.text: the site of service (as defined by the Secretary); or

children.label: II
children.text: an attestation submitted by the eligible professional.

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: iv Sunset
children.text: Clause (ii) shall no longer apply as of the first year that begins more than 3 years after the date on which the Secretary determines, through notice and comment rulemaking, that certified EHR technology applicable to the ambulatory surgical center setting is available.

children.heading: Covered professional services
children.label: i Covered professional services
children.text: The term “covered professional services” has the meaning given such term in subsection (k)(3).

children.heading: EHR reporting period
children.label: ii EHR reporting period
children.text: The term “EHR reporting period” means, with respect to a year, a period (or periods) specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Eligible professional
children.label: iii Eligible professional
children.text: The term “eligible professional” means a physician, as defined in .section 1395x(r) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: With respect to covered professional services furnished by an eligible professional during each of 2015 through 2018, if the eligible professional does not satisfactorily submit data on quality measures for covered professional services for the quality reporting period for the year (as determined under subsection (m)(3)(A)), the fee schedule amount for such services furnished by such professional during the year (including the fee schedule amount for purposes of determining a payment based on such amount) shall be equal to the applicable percent of the fee schedule amount that would otherwise apply to such services under this subsection (determined after application of paragraphs (3), (5), and (7), but without regard to this paragraph).

children.label: I
children.text: for 2015, 98.5 percent; and

children.label: II
children.text: for 2016, 2017, and 2018, 98 percent.

children.heading: Physician reporting system rules
children.label: i Physician reporting system rules
children.text: Paragraphs (5), (6), and (8) of subsection (k) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph in the same manner as they apply for purposes of such subsection.

children.heading: Incentive payment validation rules
children.label: ii Incentive payment validation rules
children.text: Clauses (ii) and (iii) of subsection (m)(5)(D) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph in a similar manner as they apply for purposes of such subsection.

children.heading: Eligible professional; covered professional services
children.label: i Eligible professional; covered professional services
children.text: The terms “eligible professional” and “covered professional services” have the meanings given such terms in subsection (k)(3).

children.heading: Physician reporting system
children.label: ii Physician reporting system
children.text: The term “physician reporting system” means the system established under subsection (k).

children.heading: Quality reporting period
children.label: iii Quality reporting period
children.text: The term “quality reporting period” means, with respect to a year, a period specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Information reporting on services included in global surgical packages
children.label: 9 Information reporting on services included in global surgical packages
children.text: With respect to services for which a physician is required to report information in accordance with subsection (c)(8)(B)(i), the Secretary may through rulemaking delay payment of 5 percent of the amount that would otherwise be payable under the physician fee schedule under this section for such services until the information so required is reported.

children.label: A
children.text: the relative value for the service (as determined in subsection (c)(2)),

children.label: B
children.text: the conversion factor (established under subsection (d)) for the year, and

children.label: C
children.text: the geographic adjustment factor (established under subsection (e)(2)) for the service for the fee schedule area.

children.heading: Treatment of radiology services and anesthesia services
children.label: 2 Treatment of radiology services and anesthesia services

children.heading: Radiology services
children.label: A Radiology services
children.text: With respect to radiology services (including radiologist services, as defined in ), the Secretary shall base the relative values on the relative value scale developed under , with appropriate modifications of the relative values to assure that the relative values established for radiology services which are similar or related to other physicians’ services are consistent with the relative values established for those similar or related services.section 1395m(b)(6) of this titlesection 1395m(b)(1)(A) of this title

children.heading: Anesthesia services
children.label: B Anesthesia services
children.text: In establishing the fee schedule for anesthesia services for which a relative value guide has been established under section 4048(b) of the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1987, the Secretary shall use, to the extent practicable, such relative value guide, with appropriate adjustment of the conversion factor, in a manner to assure that the fee schedule amounts for anesthesia services are consistent with the fee schedule amounts for other services determined by the Secretary to be of comparable value. In applying the previous sentence, the Secretary shall adjust the conversion factor by geographic adjustment factors in the same manner as such adjustment is made under paragraph (1)(C).

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: C Consultation
children.text: The Secretary shall consult with the Physician Payment Review Commission and organizations representing physicians or suppliers who furnish radiology services and anesthesia services in applying subparagraphs (A) and (B).

children.label: A
children.text: shall make separate payment under this section for the interpretation of electrocardiograms performed or ordered to be performed as part of or in conjunction with a visit to or a consultation with a physician, and

children.label: B
children.text: shall adjust the relative values established for visits and consultations under subsection (c) so as not to include relative value units for interpretations of electrocardiograms in the relative value for visits and consultations.

children.label: i
children.text: the technical component (including the technical component portion of a global fee) of the service established for a year under the fee schedule described in paragraph (1) without application of the geographic adjustment factor described in paragraph (1)(C), exceeds

children.label: ii
children.text: the Medicare OPD fee schedule amount established under the prospective payment system for hospital outpatient department services under paragraph (3)(D) of section 1395(t) of this title for such service for such year, determined without regard to geographic adjustment under paragraph (2)(D) of such section,l

children.heading: Imaging services described
children.label: B Imaging services described
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), imaging services described in this subparagraph are imaging and computer-assisted imaging services, including X-ray, ultrasound (including echocardiography), nuclear medicine (including positron emission tomography), magnetic resonance imaging, computed tomography, and fluoroscopy, but excluding diagnostic and screening mammography, and for 2010, 2011, and the first 2 months of 2012, dual-energy x-ray absorptiometry services (as described in paragraph (6)).

children.heading: Adjustment in imaging utilization rate
children.label: C Adjustment in imaging utilization rate
children.text: With respect to fee schedules established for 2011, 2012, and 2013, in the methodology for determining practice expense relative value units for expensive diagnostic imaging equipment under the final rule published by the Secretary in the Federal Register on (42 CFR 410 et al.), the Secretary shall use a 75 percent assumption instead of the utilization rates otherwise established in such final rule. With respect to fee schedules established for 2014 and subsequent years, in such methodology, the Secretary shall use a 90 percent utilization rate.November 25, 2009

children.heading: Adjustment in technical component discount on single-session imaging involving consecutive body parts
children.label: D Adjustment in technical component discount on single-session imaging involving consecutive body parts
children.text: For services furnished on or after , the Secretary shall increase the reduction in payments attributable to the multiple procedure payment reduction applicable to the technical component for imaging under the final rule published by the Secretary in the Federal Register on (part 405 of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations) from 25 percent to 50 percent.July 1, 2010November 21, 2005

children.heading: Treatment of intensive cardiac rehabilitation program
children.label: 5 Treatment of intensive cardiac rehabilitation program

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of an intensive cardiac rehabilitation program described in , the Secretary shall substitute the Medicare OPD fee schedule amount established under the prospective payment system for hospital outpatient department service under paragraph (3)(D) of section 1395(t) of this title for cardiac rehabilitation (under HCPCS codes 93797 and 93798 for calendar year 2007, or any succeeding HCPCS codes for cardiac rehabilitation).section 1395x(eee)(4) of this titlel

children.heading: Definition of session
children.label: B Definition of session
children.text: Each of the services described in subparagraphs (A) through (E) of , when furnished for one hour, is a separate session of intensive cardiac rehabilitation.section 1395x(eee)(3) of this title

children.heading: Multiple sessions per day
children.label: C Multiple sessions per day
children.text: Payment may be made for up to 6 sessions per day of the series of 72 one-hour sessions of intensive cardiac rehabilitation services described in .section 1395x(eee)(4)(B) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the relative value for the service (as determined in subsection (c)(2)) for 2006;

children.label: B
children.text: the conversion factor (established under subsection (d)) for 2006; and

children.label: C
children.text: the geographic adjustment factor (established under subsection (e)(2)) for the service for the fee schedule area for 2010, 2011, and the first 2 months of 2012, respectively.

children.heading: Adjustment in discount for certain multiple therapy services
children.label: 7 Adjustment in discount for certain multiple therapy services
children.text: In the case of therapy services furnished on or after , and before , and for which payment is made under fee schedules established under this section, instead of the 25 percent multiple procedure payment reduction specified in the final rule published by the Secretary in the Federal Register on , the reduction percentage shall be 20 percent. In the case of such services furnished on or after , and for which payment is made under such fee schedules, instead of the 25 percent multiple procedure payment reduction specified in such final rule, the reduction percentage shall be 50 percent.January 1, 2011April 1, 2013November 29, 2010April 1, 2013

children.heading: Encouraging care management for individuals with chronic care needs
children.label: 8 Encouraging care management for individuals with chronic care needs

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In order to encourage the management of care for individuals with chronic care needs the Secretary shall, subject to subparagraph (B), make payment (as the Secretary determines to be appropriate) under this section for chronic care management services furnished on or after , by a physician (as defined in ), physician assistant or nurse practitioner (as defined in ), clinical nurse specialist (as defined in ), or certified nurse midwife (as defined in ).January 1, 2015section 1395x(r)(1) of this titlesection 1395x(aa)(5)(A) of this titlesection 1395x(aa)(5)(B) of this titlesection 1395x(gg)(2) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: make payment to only one applicable provider for such services furnished to an individual during a period;

children.label: ii
children.text: not make payment under subparagraph (A) if such payment would be duplicative of payment that is otherwise made under this subchapter for such services; and

children.label: iii
children.text: not require that an annual wellness visit (as defined in ) or an initial preventive physical examination (as defined in ) be furnished as a condition of payment for such management services.section 1395x(hhh) of this titlesection 1395x(ww) of this title

children.heading: Special rule to incentivize transition from traditional X-ray imaging to digital radiography
children.label: 9 Special rule to incentivize transition from traditional X-ray imaging to digital radiography

children.heading: Limitation on payment for film X-ray imaging services
children.label: A Limitation on payment for film X-ray imaging services
children.text: In the case of an imaging service (including the imaging portion of a service) that is an X-ray taken using film and that is furnished during 2017 or a subsequent year, the payment amount for the technical component (including the technical component portion of a global service) of such service that would otherwise be determined under this section (without application of this paragraph and before application of any other adjustment under this section) for such year shall be reduced by 20 percent.

children.heading: Phased-in limitation on payment for computed radiography imaging services
children.label: B Phased-in limitation on payment for computed radiography imaging services
i
children.text: in the case of such a service furnished during 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, or 2022, the payment amount for the technical component (including the technical component portion of a global service) of such service that would otherwise be determined under this section (without application of this paragraph and before application of any other adjustment under this section) for such year shall be reduced by 7 percent; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of such a service furnished during 2023 or a subsequent year, the payment amount for the technical component (including the technical component portion of a global service) of such service that would otherwise be determined under this section (without application of this paragraph and before application of any other adjustment under this section) for such year shall be reduced by 10 percent.

children.heading: Computed radiography technology defined
children.label: C Computed radiography technology defined
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “computed radiography technology” means cassette-based imaging which utilizes an imaging plate to create the image involved.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: D Implementation
children.text: In order to implement this paragraph, the Secretary shall adopt appropriate mechanisms which may include use of modifiers.

children.heading: Reduction of discount in payment for professional component of multiple imaging services
children.label: 10 Reduction of discount in payment for professional component of multiple imaging services
children.text: In the case of the professional component of imaging services furnished on or after , instead of the 25 percent reduction for multiple procedures specified in the final rule published by the Secretary in the Federal Register on , as amended in the final rule published by the Secretary in the Federal Register on , the reduction percentage shall be 5 percent.January 1, 2017November 28, 2011November 16, 2012

children.heading: Special rule for certain radiation therapy services
children.label: 11 Special rule for certain radiation therapy services
children.text: The code definitions, the work relative value units under subsection (c)(2)(C)(i), and the direct inputs for the practice expense relative value units under subsection (c)(2)(C)(ii) for radiation treatment delivery and related imaging services (identified in 2016 by HCPCS G-codes G6001 through G6015) for the fee schedule established under this subsection for services furnished in 2017, 2018, and 2019 shall be the same as such definitions, units, and inputs for such services for the fee schedule established for services furnished in 2016.

children.heading: Payment for psychotherapy for crisis services furnished in an applicable site of service
children.label: 12 Payment for psychotherapy for crisis services furnished in an applicable site of service

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish new HCPCS codes under the fee schedule established under this subsection for services described in subparagraph (B) that are furnished on or after .January 1, 2024

children.heading: Services described
children.label: B Services described
children.text: The services described in this subparagraph are psychotherapy for crisis services that are a furnished in an applicable site of service.

children.heading: Amount of payment
children.label: C Amount of payment
children.text: For services described in subparagraph (B) that are furnished to an individual in a year (beginning with 2024), in lieu of the fee schedule amount that would otherwise be determined under this subsection for such year, the fee schedule amount for such services for such year shall be equal to 150 percent of the fee schedule amount for non-facility sites of service for such year determined for services identified, as of , by HCPCS codes 90839 and 90840 (and any succeeding codes).January 1, 2022

children.heading: Applicable site of service
children.label: i Applicable site of service
children.text: The term “applicable site of service” means a site of service other than a site where the facility rate under the fee schedule under this subsection applies and other than an office setting.

children.heading: Psychotherapy for crisis services
children.label: ii Psychotherapy for crisis services
children.text: The code descriptions for services described in subparagraph (B) shall be the same as the code descriptions for services identified, as of , by HCPCS codes 90839 and 90840 (and any succeeding codes), except that such new codes shall be limited to services furnished in an applicable site of service.January 1, 2022

children.heading: Determination of relative values for physicians’ services
children.label: c Determination of relative values for physicians’ services

children.heading: Division of physicians’ services into components
children.label: 1 Division of physicians’ services into components
i
children.text: include activities before and after direct patient contact, and

children.label: ii
children.text: be defined, with respect to surgical procedures, to reflect a global definition including pre-operative and post-operative physicians’ services.

children.heading: “Practice expense component” defined
children.label: B “Practice expense component” defined
children.text: The term “practice expense component” means the portion of the resources used in furnishing the service that reflects the general categories of expenses (such as office rent and wages of personnel, but excluding malpractice expenses) comprising practice expenses.

children.heading: “Malpractice component” defined
children.label: C “Malpractice component” defined
children.text: The term “malpractice component” means the portion of the resources used in furnishing the service that reflects malpractice expenses in furnishing the service.

children.heading: Combination of units for components
children.label: i Combination of units for components
children.text: The Secretary shall develop a methodology for combining the work, practice expense, and malpractice relative value units, determined under subparagraph (C), for each service in a manner to produce a single relative value for that service. Such relative values are subject to adjustment under subparagraph (F)(i) and section 13515(b) of the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1993.

children.heading: Extrapolation
children.label: ii Extrapolation
children.text: The Secretary may use extrapolation and other techniques to determine the number of relative value units for physicians’ services for which specific data are not available and shall take into account recommendations of the Physician Payment Review Commission and the results of consultations with organizations representing physicians who provide such services.

children.heading: Periodic review and adjustments in relative values
children.label: B Periodic review and adjustments in relative values

children.heading: Periodic review
children.label: i Periodic review
children.text: The Secretary, not less often than every 5 years, shall review the relative values established under this paragraph for all physicians’ services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The Secretary shall, to the extent the Secretary determines to be necessary and subject to subclause (II) and paragraph (7), adjust the number of such units to take into account changes in medical practice, coding changes, new data on relative value components, or the addition of new procedures. The Secretary shall publish an explanation of the basis for such adjustments.

children.heading: Limitation on annual adjustments
children.label: II Limitation on annual adjustments
children.text: Subject to clauses (iv) and (v), the adjustments under subclause (I) for a year may not cause the amount of expenditures under this part for the year to differ by more than $20,000,000 from the amount of expenditures under this part that would have been made if such adjustments had not been made.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: iii Consultation
children.text: The Secretary, in making adjustments under clause (ii), shall consult with the Medicare Payment Advisory Commission and organizations representing physicians.

children.heading: Exemption of certain additional expenditures from budget neutrality
children.label: iv Exemption of certain additional expenditures from budget neutrality
I
children.text: subparagraph (H) shall not be taken into account in applying clause (ii)(II) for 2004;

children.label: II
children.text: subparagraph (I) insofar as it relates to a physician fee schedule for 2005 or 2006 shall not be taken into account in applying clause (ii)(II) for drug administration services under the fee schedule for such year for a specialty described in subparagraph (I)(ii)(II);

children.label: III
children.text: subparagraph (J) insofar as it relates to a physician fee schedule for 2005 or 2006 shall not be taken into account in applying clause (ii)(II) for drug administration services under the fee schedule for such year;

children.label: IV
children.text: subsection (b)(6) shall not be taken into account in applying clause (ii)(II) for 2010, 2011, or the first 2 months of 2012;

children.label: V
children.text: subsection (t) shall not be taken into account in applying clause (ii)(II) for 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, or 2026; and

children.label: VI
children.text: subsection (b)(12) shall not be taken into account in applying clause (ii)(II) for 2024.

children.heading: Exemption of certain reduced expenditures from budget-neutrality calculation
children.label: v Exemption of certain reduced expenditures from budget-neutrality calculation

children.heading: Reduced payment for multiple imaging procedures
children.label: I Reduced payment for multiple imaging procedures
children.text: Effective for fee schedules established beginning with 2007, reduced expenditures attributable to the multiple procedure payment reduction for imaging under the final rule published by the Secretary in the Federal Register on (42 CFR 405, et al.) insofar as it relates to the physician fee schedules for 2006 and 2007.November 21, 2005

children.heading: OPD payment cap for imaging services
children.label: II OPD payment cap for imaging services
children.text: Effective for fee schedules established beginning with 2007, reduced expenditures attributable to subsection (b)(4).

children.heading: Change in utilization rate for certain imaging services
children.label: III Change in utilization rate for certain imaging services
children.text: Effective for fee schedules established beginning with 2011, reduced expenditures attributable to the changes in the utilization rate applicable to 2011 and 2014, as described in the first and second sentence, respectively, of subsection (b)(4)(C).

children.heading: , (V) Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 111–152, title I, § 1107(2)124 Stat. 1050Mar. 30, 2010
children.label: IV , (V) Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 111–152, title I, § 1107(2)124 Stat. 1050Mar. 30, 2010

children.heading: Additional reduced payment for multiple imaging procedures
children.label: VI Additional reduced payment for multiple imaging procedures
children.text: Effective for fee schedules established beginning with 2010 (but not applied for services furnished prior to ), reduced expenditures attributable to the increase in the multiple procedure payment reduction from 25 to 50 percent (as described in subsection (b)(4)(D)).July 1, 2010

children.heading: Reduced expenditures for multiple therapy services
children.label: VII Reduced expenditures for multiple therapy services
children.text: Effective for fee schedules established beginning with 2011, reduced expenditures attributable to the multiple procedure payment reduction for therapy services (as described in subsection (b)(7)).

children.heading: Reduced expenditures attributable to application of quality incentives for computed tomography
children.label: VIII Reduced expenditures attributable to application of quality incentives for computed tomography
children.text: Effective for fee schedules established beginning with 2016, reduced expenditures attributable to the application of the quality incentives for computed tomography undersection 1395m(p) of this title1So in original. Probably should be followed by a period.1

children.heading: Reductions for misvalued services if target not met
children.label: IX Reductions for misvalued services if target not met
children.text: Effective for fee schedules beginning with 2016, reduced expenditures attributable to the application of the target recapture amount described in subparagraph (O)(iii).

children.heading: Reduced expenditures attributable to incentives to transition to digital radiography
children.label: X Reduced expenditures attributable to incentives to transition to digital radiography
children.text: Effective for fee schedules established beginning with 2017, reduced expenditures attributable to subparagraph (A) of subsection (b)(9) and effective for fee schedules established beginning with 2018, reduced expenditures attributable to subparagraph (B) of such subsection.

children.heading: Discount in payment for professional component of imaging services
children.label: XI Discount in payment for professional component of imaging services
children.text: Effective for fee schedules established beginning with 2017, reduced expenditures attributable to subsection (b)(10).

children.heading: Alternative application of budget-neutrality adjustment
children.label: vi Alternative application of budget-neutrality adjustment
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (d)(9)(A), effective for fee schedules established beginning with 2009, with respect to the 5-year review of work relative value units used in fee schedules for 2007 and 2008, in lieu of continuing to apply budget-neutrality adjustments required under clause (ii) for 2007 and 2008 to work relative value units, the Secretary shall apply such budget-neutrality adjustments to the conversion factor otherwise determined for years beginning with 2009.

children.heading: Computation of relative value units for components
children.label: C Computation of relative value units for components

children.heading: Work relative value units
children.label: i Work relative value units
children.text: The Secretary shall determine a number of work relative value units for the service or group of services based on the relative resources incorporating physician time and intensity required in furnishing the service or group of services.

children.heading: Practice expense relative value units
children.label: ii Practice expense relative value units
I
children.text: the base allowed charges (as defined in subparagraph (D)) for the service, and

children.label: II
children.text: the practice expense percentage for the service (as determined under paragraph (3)(C)(ii)),

children.label: I
children.text: the base allowed charges (as defined in subparagraph (D)) for the service or group of services, and

children.label: II
children.text: the malpractice percentage for the service or group of services (as determined under paragraph (3)(C)(iii)),

children.heading: “Base allowed charges” defined
children.label: D “Base allowed charges” defined
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “base allowed charges” means, with respect to a physician’s service, the national average allowed charges for the service under this part for services furnished during 1991, as estimated by the Secretary using the most recent data available.

children.heading: Reduction in practice expense relative value units for certain services
children.label: E Reduction in practice expense relative value units for certain services
I
children.text: 1994, by 25 percent of the number by which the number of practice expense relative value units (determined for 1994 without regard to this subparagraph) exceeds the number of work relative value units determined for 1994,

children.label: II
children.text: 1995, by an additional 25 percent of such excess, and

children.label: III
children.text: 1996, by an additional 25 percent of such excess.

children.heading: Floor on reductions
children.label: ii Floor on reductions
children.text: The practice expense relative value units for a physician’s service shall not be reduced under this subparagraph to a number less than 128 percent of the number of work relative value units.

children.label: I
children.text: there are work relative value units, and

children.label: II
children.text: the number of practice expense relative value units (determined for 1994) exceeds 128 percent of the number of work relative value units (determined for such year).

children.heading: Excluded services
children.label: iv Excluded services
children.text: For purposes of clause (iii), the services described in this clause are services which the Secretary determines at least 75 percent of which are provided under this subchapter in an office setting.

children.label: i
children.text: shall reduce the relative values for all services (other than anesthesia services) established under this paragraph (and, in the case of anesthesia services, the conversion factor established by the Secretary for such services) by such percentage as the Secretary determines to be necessary so that, beginning in 1996, the amendment made by section 13514(a) of the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1993 would not result in expenditures under this section that exceed the amount of such expenditures that would have been made if such amendment had not been made, and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall reduce the amounts determined under subsection (a)(2)(B)(ii)(I) by such percentage as the Secretary determines to be required to assure that, taking into account the reductions made under clause (i), the amendment made by section 13514(a) of the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1993 would not result in expenditures under this section in 1994 that exceed the amount of such expenditures that would have been made if such amendment had not been made.

children.heading: Adjustments in relative value units for 1998
children.label: G Adjustments in relative value units for 1998
I
children.text: subject to clauses (iv) and (v), reduce the practice expense relative value units applied to any services described in clause (ii) furnished in 1998 to a number equal to 110 percent of the number of work relative value units, and

children.label: II
children.text: increase the practice expense relative value units for office visit procedure codes during 1998 by a uniform percentage which the Secretary estimates will result in an aggregate increase in payments for such services equal to the aggregate decrease in payments by reason of subclause (I).

children.label: I
children.text: there are work relative value units, and

children.label: II
children.text: the number of practice expense relative value units (determined for 1998) exceeds 110 percent of the number of work relative value units (determined for such year).

children.heading: Excluded services
children.label: iii Excluded services
children.text: For purposes of clause (ii), the services described in this clause are services which the Secretary determines at least 75 percent of which are provided under this subchapter in an office setting.

children.heading: Limitation on aggregate reallocation
children.label: iv Limitation on aggregate reallocation
children.text: If the application of clause (i)(I) would result in an aggregate amount of reductions under such clause in excess of $390,000,000, such clause shall be applied by substituting for 110 percent such greater percentage as the Secretary estimates will result in the aggregate amount of such reductions equaling $390,000,000.

children.heading: No reduction for certain services
children.label: v No reduction for certain services
children.text: Practice expense relative value units for a procedure performed in an office or in a setting out of an office shall not be reduced under clause (i) if the in-office or out-of-office practice expense relative value, respectively, for the procedure would increase under the proposed rule on resource-based practice expenses issued by the Secretary on (62 Federal Register 33158 et seq.).June 18, 1997

children.heading: Adjustments in practice expense relative value units for certain drug administration services beginning in 2004
children.label: H Adjustments in practice expense relative value units for certain drug administration services beginning in 2004
I
children.text: covers practice expenses for oncology drug administration services; and

children.label: II
children.text: meets criteria established by the Secretary for acceptance of such surveys.

children.heading: Pricing of clinical oncology nurses in practice expense methodology
children.label: ii Pricing of clinical oncology nurses in practice expense methodology
children.text: If the survey described in clause (i) includes data on wages, salaries, and compensation of clinical oncology nurses, the Secretary shall utilize such data in the methodology for determining practice expense relative value units under subsection (c).

children.heading: Work relative value units for certain drug administration services
children.label: iii Work relative value units for certain drug administration services
children.text: In establishing the relative value units under this paragraph for drug administration services described in clause (iv) furnished on or after , the Secretary shall establish work relative value units equal to the work relative value units for a level 1 office medical visit for an established patient.January 1, 2004

children.label: I
children.text: which are classified as of , within any of the following groups of procedures: therapeutic or diagnostic infusions (excluding chemotherapy); chemotherapy administration services; and therapeutic, prophylactic, or diagnostic injections;October 1, 2003

children.label: II
children.text: for which there are no work relative value units assigned under this subsection as of such date; and

children.label: III
children.text: for which national relative value units have been assigned under this subsection as of such date.

children.heading: Adjustments in practice expense relative value units for certain drug administration services beginning with 2005
children.label: I Adjustments in practice expense relative value units for certain drug administration services beginning with 2005

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In establishing the physician fee schedule under subsection (b) with respect to payments for services furnished on or after or 2006, the Secretary shall adjust the practice expense relative value units for such year consistent with clause (ii).January 1, 2005

children.heading: Use of supplemental survey data
children.label: ii Use of supplemental survey data

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Subject to subclause (II), if a specialty submits to the Secretary by not later than , for 2005, or , for 2006, data that includes expenses for the administration of drugs and biologicals for which the payment amount is determined pursuant to section 1395u() of this title, the Secretary shall use such supplemental survey data in carrying out this subparagraph for the years involved insofar as they are collected and provided by entities and organizations consistent with the criteria established by the Secretary pursuant to section 212(a) of the Medicare, Medicaid, and SCHIP Balanced Budget Refinement Act of 1999.March 1, 2004March 1, 2005o

children.heading: Limitation on specialty
children.label: II Limitation on specialty
children.text: Subclause (I) shall apply to a specialty only insofar as not less than 40 percent of payments for the specialty under this subchapter in 2002 are attributable to the administration of drugs and biologicals, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Application
children.label: III Application
children.text: This clause shall not apply with respect to a survey to which subparagraph (H)(i) applies.

children.heading: Provisions for appropriate reporting and billing for physicians’ services associated with the administration of covered outpatient drugs and biologicals
children.label: J Provisions for appropriate reporting and billing for physicians’ services associated with the administration of covered outpatient drugs and biologicals

children.heading: Evaluation of codes
children.label: i Evaluation of codes
children.text: The Secretary shall promptly evaluate existing drug administration codes for physicians’ services to ensure accurate reporting and billing for such services, taking into account levels of complexity of the administration and resource consumption.

children.heading: Use of existing processes
children.label: ii Use of existing processes
children.text: In carrying out clause (i), the Secretary shall use existing processes for the consideration of coding changes and, to the extent coding changes are made, shall use such processes in establishing relative values for such services.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: iii Implementation
children.text: In carrying out clause (i), the Secretary shall consult with representatives of physician specialties affected by the implementation of or , and shall take such steps within the Secretary’s authority to expedite such considerations under clause (ii).section 1395w–3a of this titlesection 1395w–3b of this title

children.heading: Subsequent, budget neutral adjustments permitted
children.label: iv Subsequent, budget neutral adjustments permitted
children.text: Nothing in subparagraph (H) or (I) or this subparagraph shall be construed as preventing the Secretary from providing for adjustments in practice expense relative value units under (and consistent with) subparagraph (B) for years after 2004, 2005, or 2006, respectively.

children.label: I
children.text: periodically identify services as being potentially misvalued using criteria specified in clause (ii); and

children.label: II
children.text: review and make appropriate adjustments to the relative values established under this paragraph for services identified as being potentially misvalued under subclause (I).

children.heading: Identification of potentially misvalued codes
children.label: ii Identification of potentially misvalued codes
I
children.text: Codes that have experienced the fastest growth.

children.label: II
children.text: Codes that have experienced substantial changes in practice expenses.

children.label: III
children.text: Codes that describe new technologies or services within an appropriate time period (such as 3 years) after the relative values are initially established for such codes.

children.label: IV
children.text: Codes which are multiple codes that are frequently billed in conjunction with furnishing a single service.

children.label: V
children.text: Codes with low relative values, particularly those that are often billed multiple times for a single treatment.

children.label: VI
children.text: Codes that have not been subject to review since implementation of the fee schedule.

children.label: VII
children.text: Codes that account for the majority of spending under the physician fee schedule.

children.label: VIII
children.text: Codes for services that have experienced a substantial change in the hospital length of stay or procedure time.

children.label: IX
children.text: Codes for which there may be a change in the typical site of service since the code was last valued.

children.label: X
children.text: Codes for which there is a significant difference in payment for the same service between different sites of service.

children.label: XI
children.text: Codes for which there may be anomalies in relative values within a family of codes.

children.label: XII
children.text: Codes for services where there may be efficiencies when a service is furnished at the same time as other services.

children.label: XIII
children.text: Codes with high intra-service work per unit of time.

children.label: XIV
children.text: Codes with high practice expense relative value units.

children.label: XV
children.text: Codes with high cost supplies.

children.label: XVI
children.text: Codes as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: The Secretary may use existing processes to receive recommendations on the review and appropriate adjustment of potentially misvalued services described in clause (i)(II).

children.label: II
children.text: The Secretary may conduct surveys, other data collection activities, studies, or other analyses as the Secretary determines to be appropriate to facilitate the review and appropriate adjustment described in clause (i)(II).

children.label: III
children.text: The Secretary may use analytic contractors to identify and analyze services identified under clause (i)(I), conduct surveys or collect data, and make recommendations on the review and appropriate adjustment of services described in clause (i)(II).

children.label: IV
children.text: The Secretary may coordinate the review and appropriate adjustment described in clause (i)(II) with the periodic review described in subparagraph (B).

children.label: V
children.text: As part of the review and adjustment described in clause (i)(II), including with respect to codes with low relative values described in clause (ii), the Secretary may make appropriate coding revisions (including using existing processes for consideration of coding changes) which may include consolidation of individual services into bundled codes for payment under the fee schedule under subsection (b).

children.label: VI
children.text: The provisions of subparagraph (B)(ii)(II) and paragraph (7) shall apply to adjustments to relative value units made pursuant to this subparagraph in the same manner as such provisions apply to adjustments under subparagraph (B)(ii)(I).

children.heading: Treatment of certain radiation therapy services
children.label: iv Treatment of certain radiation therapy services
children.text: Radiation treatment delivery and related imaging services identified under subsection (b)(11) shall not be considered as potentially misvalued services for purposes of this subparagraph and subparagraph (O) for 2017, 2018, and 2019.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a process to validate relative value units under the fee schedule under subsection (b).

children.heading: Components and elements of work
children.label: ii Components and elements of work
children.text: The process described in clause (i) may include validation of work elements (such as time, mental effort and professional judgment, technical skill and physical effort, and stress due to risk) involved with furnishing a service and may include validation of the pre-, post-, and intra-service components of work.

children.heading: Scope of codes
children.label: iii Scope of codes
children.text: The validation of work relative value units shall include a sampling of codes for services that is the same as the codes listed under subparagraph (K)(ii).

children.heading: Methods
children.label: iv Methods
children.text: The Secretary may conduct the validation under this subparagraph using methods described in subclauses (I) through (V) of subparagraph (K)(iii) as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Adjustments
children.label: v Adjustments
children.text: The Secretary shall make appropriate adjustments to the work relative value units under the fee schedule under subsection (b). The provisions of subparagraph (B)(ii)(II) shall apply to adjustments to relative value units made pursuant to this subparagraph in the same manner as such provisions apply to adjustments under subparagraph (B)(ii)(II).

children.heading: Authority to collect and use information on physicians’ services in the determination of relative values
children.label: M Authority to collect and use information on physicians’ services in the determination of relative values

children.heading: Collection of information
children.label: i Collection of information
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary may collect or obtain information on the resources directly or indirectly related to furnishing services for which payment is made under the fee schedule established under subsection (b). Such information may be collected or obtained from any eligible professional or any other source.

children.heading: Use of information
children.label: ii Use of information
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, subject to clause (v), the Secretary may (as the Secretary determines appropriate) use information collected or obtained pursuant to clause (i) in the determination of relative values for services under this section.

children.label: I
children.text: Time involved in furnishing services.

children.label: II
children.text: Amounts and types of practice expense inputs involved with furnishing services.

children.label: III
children.text: Prices (net of any discounts) for practice expense inputs, which may include paid invoice prices or other documentation or records.

children.label: IV
children.text: Overhead and accounting information for practices of physicians and other suppliers.

children.label: V
children.text: Any other element that would improve the valuation of services under this section.

children.label: I
children.text: Surveys of physicians, other suppliers, providers of services, manufacturers, and vendors.

children.label: II
children.text: Surgical logs, billing systems, or other practice or facility records.

children.label: III
children.text: Electronic health records.

children.label: IV
children.text: Any other mechanism determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Transparency of use of information
children.label: v Transparency of use of information

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Subject to subclauses (II) and (III), if the Secretary uses information collected or obtained under this subparagraph in the determination of relative values under this subsection, the Secretary shall disclose the information source and discuss the use of such information in such determination of relative values through notice and comment rulemaking.

children.heading: Thresholds for use
children.label: II Thresholds for use
children.text: The Secretary may establish thresholds in order to use such information, including the exclusion of information collected or obtained from eligible professionals who use very high resources (as determined by the Secretary) in furnishing a service.

children.heading: Disclosure of information
children.label: III Disclosure of information
children.text: The Secretary shall make aggregate information available under this subparagraph but shall not disclose information in a form or manner that identifies an eligible professional or a group practice, or information collected or obtained pursuant to a nondisclosure agreement.

children.heading: Incentive to participate
children.label: vi Incentive to participate
children.text: The Secretary may provide for such payments under this part to an eligible professional that submits such solicited information under this subparagraph as the Secretary determines appropriate in order to compensate such eligible professional for such submission. Such payments shall be provided in a form and manner specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: vii Administration
children.text: Chapter 35 of title 44 shall not apply to information collected or obtained under this subparagraph.

children.heading: Definition of eligible professional
children.label: viii Definition of eligible professional
children.text: In this subparagraph, the term “eligible professional” has the meaning given such term in subsection (k)(3)(B).

children.heading: Funding
children.label: ix Funding
