children.text: For purposes of carrying out this subparagraph, in addition to funds otherwise appropriated, the Secretary shall provide for the transfer, from the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under , of $2,000,000 to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services Program Management Account for each fiscal year beginning with fiscal year 2014. Amounts transferred under the preceding sentence for a fiscal year shall be available until expended.section 1395t of this title

children.heading: Authority for alternative approaches to establishing practice expense relative values
children.label: N Authority for alternative approaches to establishing practice expense relative values
children.text: The Secretary may establish or adjust practice expense relative values under this subsection using cost, charge, or other data from suppliers or providers of services, including information collected or obtained under subparagraph (M).

children.heading: Target for relative value adjustments for misvalued services
children.label: O Target for relative value adjustments for misvalued services

children.heading: Determination of net reduction in expenditures
children.label: i Determination of net reduction in expenditures
children.text: For each year, the Secretary shall determine the estimated net reduction in expenditures under the fee schedule under this section with respect to the year as a result of adjustments to the relative values established under this paragraph for misvalued codes.

children.heading: Budget neutral redistribution of funds if target met and counting overages towards the target for the succeeding year
children.label: ii Budget neutral redistribution of funds if target met and counting overages towards the target for the succeeding year
I
children.text: reduced expenditures attributable to such adjustments shall be redistributed for the year in a budget neutral manner in accordance with subparagraph (B)(ii)(II); and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount by which such reduced expenditures exceeds the target for the year shall be treated as a reduction in expenditures described in clause (i) for the succeeding year, for purposes of determining whether the target has or has not been met under this subparagraph with respect to that year.

children.heading: Exemption from budget neutrality if target not met
children.label: iii Exemption from budget neutrality if target not met
children.text: If the estimated net reduction in expenditures determined under clause (i) for the year is less than the target for the year, reduced expenditures in an amount equal to the target recapture amount shall not be taken into account in applying subparagraph (B)(ii)(II) with respect to fee schedules beginning with 2016.

children.label: I
children.text: the target for the year; and

children.label: II
children.text: the estimated net reduction in expenditures determined under clause (i) for the year.

children.heading: Target
children.label: v Target
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, with respect to a year, the target is calculated as 0.5 percent (or, for 2016, 1.0 percent) of the estimated amount of expenditures under the fee schedule under this section for the year.

children.heading: Division of services by specialty
children.label: A Division of services by specialty
children.text: For each physician’s service or class of physicians’ services, the Secretary shall determine the average percentage of each such service or class of services that is performed, nationwide, under this part by physicians in each of the different physician specialties (as identified by the Secretary).

children.heading: Division of specialty by component
children.label: B Division of specialty by component
children.text: The Secretary shall determine the average percentage division of resources, among the work component, the practice expense component, and the malpractice component, used by physicians in each of such specialties in furnishing physicians’ services. Such percentages shall be based on national data that describe the elements of physician practice costs and revenues, by physician specialty. The Secretary may use extrapolation and other techniques to determine practice costs and revenues for specialties for which adequate data are not available.

children.label: I
children.text: the average percentage division for the work component for each physician specialty (determined under subparagraph (B)), multiplied by

children.label: II
children.text: the proportion (determined under subparagraph (A)) of such service (or services) performed by physicians in that specialty.

children.label: I
children.text: the average percentage division for the practice expense component for each physician specialty (determined under subparagraph (B)), multiplied by

children.label: II
children.text: the proportion (determined under subparagraph (A)) of such service (or services) performed by physicians in that specialty.

children.label: I
children.text: the average percentage division for the malpractice component for each physician specialty (determined under subparagraph (B)), multiplied by

children.label: II
children.text: the proportion (determined under subparagraph (A)) of such service (or services) performed by physicians in that specialty.

children.heading: Periodic recomputation
children.label: D Periodic recomputation
children.text: The Secretary may, from time to time, provide for the recomputation of work percentages, practice expense percentages, and malpractice percentages determined under this paragraph.

children.heading: Ancillary policies
children.label: 4 Ancillary policies
children.text: The Secretary may establish ancillary policies (with respect to the use of modifiers, local codes, and other matters) as may be necessary to implement this section.

children.heading: Coding
children.label: 5 Coding
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a uniform procedure coding system for the coding of all physicians’ services. The Secretary shall provide for an appropriate coding structure for visits and consultations. The Secretary may incorporate the use of time in the coding for visits and consultations. The Secretary, in establishing such coding system, shall consult with the Physician Payment Review Commission and other organizations representing physicians.

children.heading: No variation for specialists
children.label: 6 No variation for specialists
children.text: The Secretary may not vary the conversion factor or the number of relative value units for a physicians’ service based on whether the physician furnishing the service is a specialist or based on the type of specialty of the physician.

children.heading: Phase-in of significant relative value unit (RVU) reductions
children.label: 7 Phase-in of significant relative value unit (RVU) reductions
children.text: Effective for fee schedules established beginning with 2016, for services that are not new or revised codes, if the total relative value units for a service for a year would otherwise be decreased by an estimated amount equal to or greater than 20 percent as compared to the total relative value units for the previous year, the applicable adjustments in work, practice expense, and malpractice relative value units shall be phased-in over a 2-year period.

children.heading: Prohibition of implementation of rule regarding global surgical packages
children.label: A Prohibition of implementation of rule regarding global surgical packages

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall not implement the policy established in the final rule published on (79 Fed. Reg. 67548 et seq.), that requires the transition of all 10-day and 90-day global surgery packages to 0-day global periods.November 13, 2014

children.heading: Construction
children.label: ii Construction
children.text: Nothing in clause (i) shall be construed to prevent the Secretary from revaluing misvalued codes for specific surgical services or assigning values to new or revised codes for surgical services.

children.heading: Collection of data on services included in global surgical packages
children.label: B Collection of data on services included in global surgical packages

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), the Secretary shall through rulemaking develop and implement a process to gather, from a representative sample of physicians, beginning not later than , information needed to value surgical services. Such information shall include the number and level of medical visits furnished during the global period and other items and services related to the surgery and furnished during the global period, as appropriate. Such information shall be reported on claims at the end of the global period or in another manner specified by the Secretary. For purposes of carrying out this paragraph (other than clause (iii)), the Secretary shall transfer from the Federal Supplemental Medical Insurance Trust Fund under $2,000,000 to the Center for Medicare & Medicaid Services Program Management Account for fiscal year 2015. Amounts transferred under the previous sentence shall remain available until expended.January 1, 2017section 1395t of this title

children.heading: Reassessment and potential sunset
children.label: ii Reassessment and potential sunset
children.text: Every 4 years, the Secretary shall reassess the value of the information collected pursuant to clause (i). Based on such a reassessment and by regulation, the Secretary may discontinue the requirement for collection of information under such clause if the Secretary determines that the Secretary has adequate information from other sources, such as qualified clinical data registries, surgical logs, billing systems or other practice or facility records, and electronic health records, in order to accurately value global surgical services under this section.

children.heading: Inspector general audit
children.label: iii Inspector general audit
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services shall audit a sample of the information reported under clause (i) to verify the accuracy of the information so reported.

children.heading: Improving accuracy of pricing for surgical services
children.label: C Improving accuracy of pricing for surgical services
children.text: For years beginning with 2019, the Secretary shall use the information reported under subparagraph (B)(i) as appropriate and other available data for the purpose of improving the accuracy of valuation of surgical services under the physician fee schedule under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The conversion factor for each year shall be the conversion factor established under this subsection for the previous year (or, in the case of 1992, specified in subparagraph (B)) adjusted by the update (established under paragraph (3)) for the year involved (for years before 2001) and, for years beginning with 2001 and ending with 2025, multiplied by the update (established under paragraph (4) or a subsequent paragraph) for the year involved. There shall be two separate conversion factors for each year beginning with 2026, one for items and services furnished by a qualifying APM participant (as defined in section 1395(z)(2) of this title) (referred to in this subsection as the “qualifying APM conversion factor”) and the other for other items and services (referred to in this subsection as the “nonqualifying APM conversion factor”), equal to the respective conversion factor for the previous year (or, in the case of 2026, equal to the single conversion factor for 2025) multiplied by the update established under paragraph (20) for such respective conversion factor for such year.l

children.heading: Special provision for 1992
children.label: B Special provision for 1992
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the conversion factor specified in this subparagraph is a conversion factor (determined by the Secretary) which, if this section were to apply during 1991 using such conversion factor, would result in the same aggregate amount of payments under this part for physicians’ services as the estimated aggregate amount of the payments under this part for such services in 1991.

children.heading: Special rules for 1998
children.label: C Special rules for 1998
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (D), the single conversion factor for 1998 under this subsection shall be the conversion factor for primary care services for 1997, increased by the Secretary’s estimate of the weighted average of the three separate updates that would otherwise occur were it not for the enactment of chapter 1 of subtitle F of title IV of the Balanced Budget Act of 1997.

children.heading: Special rules for anesthesia services
children.label: D Special rules for anesthesia services
children.text: The separate conversion factor for anesthesia services for a year shall be equal to 46 percent of the single conversion factor (or, beginning with 2026, applicable conversion factor) established for other physicians’ services, except as adjusted for changes in work, practice expense, or malpractice relative value units.

children.heading: Publication and dissemination of information
children.label: E Publication and dissemination of information
i
children.text: cause to have published in the Federal Register not later than November 1 of each year (beginning with 2000) the conversion factor which will apply to physicians’ services for the succeeding year, the update determined under paragraph (4) for such succeeding year, and the allowed expenditures under such paragraph for such succeeding year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: make available to the Medicare Payment Advisory Commission and the public by March 1 of each year (beginning with 2000) an estimate of the sustainable growth rate and of the conversion factor which will apply to physicians’ services for the succeeding year and data used in making such estimate.

children.label: i
children.text: 1 plus the Secretary’s estimate of the percentage increase in the MEI (as defined in ) for the year (divided by 100), andsection 1395u(i)(3) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: 1 plus the Secretary’s estimate of the update adjustment factor for the year (divided by 100),

children.label: i
children.text: the difference between (I) the sum of the allowed expenditures for physicians’ services (as determined under subparagraph (C)) for the period beginning , and ending on March 31 of the year involved, and (II) the amount of actual expenditures for physicians’ services furnished during the period beginning , and ending on March 31 of the preceding year; divided byApril 1, 1997April 1, 1997

children.label: ii
children.text: the actual expenditures for physicians’ services for the 12-month period ending on March 31 of the preceding year, increased by the sustainable growth rate under subsection (f) for the fiscal year which begins during such 12-month period.

children.label: i
children.text: 1997 is equal to the actual expenditures for physicians’ services furnished during such 12-month period, as estimated by the Secretary; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a subsequent year is equal to the allowed expenditures for physicians’ services for the previous year, increased by the sustainable growth rate under subsection (f) for the fiscal year which begins during such 12-month period.

children.heading: Restriction on variation from medicare economic index
children.label: D Restriction on variation from medicare economic index
i
children.text: greater than 100 times the following amount: (1.03 + (MEI percentage/100)) −1; or

children.label: ii
children.text: less than 100 times the following amount: (0.93 + (MEI percentage/100)) −1,

children.heading: Update for years beginning with 2001 and ending with 2014
children.label: 4 Update for years beginning with 2001 and ending with 2014
i
children.text: 1 plus the Secretary’s estimate of the percentage increase in the MEI (as defined in ) for the year (divided by 100); andsection 1395u(i)(3) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: 1 plus the Secretary’s estimate of the update adjustment factor under subparagraph (B) for the year.

children.label: I
children.text: computing the difference (which may be positive or negative) between the amount of the allowed expenditures for physicians’ services for the prior year (as determined under subparagraph (C)) and the amount of the actual expenditures for such services for that year;

children.label: II
children.text: dividing that difference by the amount of the actual expenditures for such services for that year; and

children.label: III
children.text: multiplying that quotient by 0.75.

children.label: I
children.text: computing the difference (which may be positive or negative) between the amount of the allowed expenditures for physicians’ services (as determined under subparagraph (C)) from , through the end of the prior year and the amount of the actual expenditures for such services during that period;April 1, 1996

children.label: II
children.text: dividing that difference by actual expenditures for such services for the prior year as increased by the sustainable growth rate under subsection (f) for the year for which the update adjustment factor is to be determined; and

children.label: III
children.text: multiplying that quotient by 0.33.

children.heading: Period up toApril 1, 1999
children.label: i Period up toApril 1, 1999
children.text: The allowed expenditures for physicians’ services for a period before , shall be the amount of the allowed expenditures for such period as determined under paragraph (3)(C).April 1, 1999

children.heading: Transition to calendar year allowed expenditures
children.label: ii Transition to calendar year allowed expenditures
I
children.text: the 9-month period beginning , shall be the Secretary’s estimate of the amount of the allowed expenditures that would be permitted under paragraph (3)(C) for such period; andApril 1, 1999

children.label: II
children.text: the year of 1999, shall be the Secretary’s estimate of the amount of the allowed expenditures that would be permitted under paragraph (3)(C) for such year.

children.heading: Years beginning with 2000
children.label: iii Years beginning with 2000
children.text: The allowed expenditures for a year (beginning with 2000) is equal to the allowed expenditures for physicians’ services for the previous year, increased by the sustainable growth rate under subsection (f) for the year involved.

children.heading: Restriction on update adjustment factor
children.label: D Restriction on update adjustment factor
children.text: The update adjustment factor determined under subparagraph (B) for a year may not be less than −0.07 or greater than 0.03.

children.heading: Recalculation of allowed expenditures for updates beginning with 2001
children.label: E Recalculation of allowed expenditures for updates beginning with 2001
children.text: For purposes of determining the update adjustment factor for a year beginning with 2001, the Secretary shall recompute the allowed expenditures for previous periods beginning on or after , consistent with subsection (f)(3).April 1, 1999

children.heading: Transitional adjustment designed to provide for budget neutrality
children.label: F Transitional adjustment designed to provide for budget neutrality
i
children.text: for each of 2001, 2002, 2003, and 2004, of −0.2 percent; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for 2005 of +0.8 percent.

children.heading: Update for 2004 and 2005
children.label: 5 Update for 2004 and 2005
children.text: The update to the single conversion factor established in paragraph (1)(C) for each of 2004 and 2005 shall be not less than 1.5 percent.

children.heading: Update for 2006
children.label: 6 Update for 2006
children.text: The update to the single conversion factor established in paragraph (1)(C) for 2006 shall be 0 percent.

children.label: i
children.text: 1 plus the Secretary’s estimate of the percentage increase in the MEI (as defined in ) for 2007 (divided by 100); andsection 1395u(i)(3) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: 1 plus the Secretary’s estimate of the update adjustment factor under paragraph (4)(B) for 2007.

children.heading: No effect on computation of conversion factor for 2008
children.label: B No effect on computation of conversion factor for 2008
children.text: The conversion factor under this subsection shall be computed under paragraph (1)(A) for 2008 as if subparagraph (A) had never applied.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (7)(B), in lieu of the update to the single conversion factor established in paragraph (1)(C) that would otherwise apply for 2008, the update to the single conversion factor shall be 0.5 percent.

children.heading: No effect on computation of conversion factor for 2009
children.label: B No effect on computation of conversion factor for 2009
children.text: The conversion factor under this subsection shall be computed under paragraph (1)(A) for 2009 and subsequent years as if subparagraph (A) had never applied.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (7)(B) and (8)(B), in lieu of the update to the single conversion factor established in paragraph (1)(C) that would otherwise apply for 2009, the update to the single conversion factor shall be 1.1 percent.

children.heading: No effect on computation of conversion factor for 2010 and subsequent years
children.label: B No effect on computation of conversion factor for 2010 and subsequent years
children.text: The conversion factor under this subsection shall be computed under paragraph (1)(A) for 2010 and subsequent years as if subparagraph (A) had never applied.

children.heading: Update for January through May of 2010
children.label: 10 Update for January through May of 2010

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (7)(B), (8)(B), and (9)(B), in lieu of the update to the single conversion factor established in paragraph (1)(C) that would otherwise apply for 2010 for the period beginning on , and ending on , the update to the single conversion factor shall be 0 percent for 2010.January 1, 2010May 31, 2010

children.heading: No effect on computation of conversion factor for remaining portion of 2010 and subsequent years
children.label: B No effect on computation of conversion factor for remaining portion of 2010 and subsequent years
children.text: The conversion factor under this subsection shall be computed under paragraph (1)(A) for the period beginning on , and ending on , and for 2011 and subsequent years as if subparagraph (A) had never applied.June 1, 2010December 31, 2010

children.heading: Update for June through December of 2010
children.label: 11 Update for June through December of 2010

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (7)(B), (8)(B), (9)(B), and (10)(B), in lieu of the update to the single conversion factor established in paragraph (1)(C) that would otherwise apply for 2010 for the period beginning on , and ending on , the update to the single conversion factor shall be 2.2 percent.June 1, 2010December 31, 2010

children.heading: No effect on computation of conversion factor for 2011 and subsequent years
children.label: B No effect on computation of conversion factor for 2011 and subsequent years
children.text: The conversion factor under this subsection shall be computed under paragraph (1)(A) for 2011 and subsequent years as if subparagraph (A) had never applied.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (7)(B), (8)(B), (9)(B), (10)(B), and (11)(B), in lieu of the update to the single conversion factor established in paragraph (1)(C) that would otherwise apply for 2011, the update to the single conversion factor shall be 0 percent.

children.heading: No effect on computation of conversion factor for 2012 and subsequent years
children.label: B No effect on computation of conversion factor for 2012 and subsequent years
children.text: The conversion factor under this subsection shall be computed under paragraph (1)(A) for 2012 and subsequent years as if subparagraph (A) had never applied.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (7)(B), (8)(B), (9)(B), (10)(B), (11)(B), and (12)(B), in lieu of the update to the single conversion factor established in paragraph (1)(C) that would otherwise apply for 2012, the update to the single conversion factor shall be zero percent.

children.heading: No effect on computation of conversion factor for 2013 and subsequent years
children.label: B No effect on computation of conversion factor for 2013 and subsequent years
children.text: The conversion factor under this subsection shall be computed under paragraph (1)(A) for 2013 and subsequent years as if subparagraph (A) had never applied.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (7)(B), (8)(B), (9)(B), (10)(B), (11)(B), (12)(B), and (13)(B), in lieu of the update to the single conversion factor established in paragraph (1)(C) that would otherwise apply for 2013, the update to the single conversion factor for such year shall be zero percent.

children.heading: No effect on computation of conversion factor for 2014 and subsequent years
children.label: B No effect on computation of conversion factor for 2014 and subsequent years
children.text: The conversion factor under this subsection shall be computed under paragraph (1)(A) for 2014 and subsequent years as if subparagraph (A) had never applied.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (7)(B), (8)(B), (9)(B), (10)(B), (11)(B), (12)(B), (13)(B), and (14)(B), in lieu of the update to the single conversion factor established in paragraph (1)(C) that would otherwise apply for 2014, the update to the single conversion factor shall be 0.5 percent.

children.heading: No effect on computation of conversion factor for subsequent years
children.label: B No effect on computation of conversion factor for subsequent years
children.text: The conversion factor under this subsection shall be computed under paragraph (1)(A) for 2015 and subsequent years as if subparagraph (A) had never applied.

children.heading: Update for January through June of 2015
children.label: 16 Update for January through June of 2015
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (7)(B), (8)(B), (9)(B), (10)(B), (11)(B), (12)(B), (13)(B), (14)(B), and (15)(B), in lieu of the update to the single conversion factor established in paragraph (1)(C) that would otherwise apply for 2015 for the period beginning on , and ending on , the update to the single conversion factor shall be 0.0 percent.January 1, 2015June 30, 2015

children.heading: Update for July through December of 2015
children.label: 17 Update for July through December of 2015
children.text: The update to the single conversion factor established in paragraph (1)(C) for the period beginning on , and ending on , shall be 0.5 percent.July 1, 2015December 31, 2015

children.label: A
children.text: for 2016 and each subsequent year through 2018 shall be 0.5 percent; and

children.label: B
children.text: for 2019 shall be 0.25 percent.

children.heading: Update for 2020 through 2025
children.label: 19 Update for 2020 through 2025
children.text: The update to the single conversion factor established in paragraph (1)(C) for 2020 and each subsequent year through 2025 shall be 0.0 percent.

children.heading: Update for 2026 and subsequent years
children.label: 20 Update for 2026 and subsequent years
children.text: For 2026 and each subsequent year, the update to the qualifying APM conversion factor established under paragraph (1)(A) is 0.75 percent, and the update to the nonqualifying APM conversion factor established under such paragraph is 0.25 percent.

children.label: i
children.text: an index which reflects the relative costs of the mix of goods and services comprising practice expenses (other than malpractice expenses) in the different fee schedule areas compared to the national average of such costs,

children.label: ii
children.text: an index which reflects the relative costs of malpractice expenses in the different fee schedule areas compared to the national average of such costs, and

children.label: iii
children.text: an index which reflects ¼ of the difference between the relative value of physicians’ work effort in each of the different fee schedule areas and the national average of such work effort.

children.heading: Class-specific geographic cost-of-practice indices
children.label: B Class-specific geographic cost-of-practice indices
children.text: The Secretary may establish more than one index under subparagraph (A)(i) in the case of classes of physicians’ services, if, because of differences in the mix of goods and services comprising practice expenses for the different classes of services, the application of a single index under such clause to different classes of such services would be substantially inequitable.

children.heading: Periodic review and adjustments in geographic adjustment factors
children.label: C Periodic review and adjustments in geographic adjustment factors
children.text: The Secretary, not less often than every 3 years, shall, in consultation with appropriate representatives of physicians, review the indices established under subparagraph (A) and the geographic index values applied under this subsection for all fee schedule areas. Based on such review, the Secretary may revise such index and adjust such index values, except that, if more than 1 year has elasped since the date of the last previous adjustment, the adjustment to be applied in the first year of the next adjustment shall be ½ of the adjustment that otherwise would be made.2So in original. Probably should be “elapsed”.2

children.heading: Use of recent data
children.label: D Use of recent data
children.text: In establishing indices and index values under this paragraph, the Secretary shall use the most recent data available relating to practice expenses, malpractice expenses, and physician work effort in different fee schedule areas.

children.heading: Floor at 1.0 on work geographic index
children.label: E Floor at 1.0 on work geographic index
children.text: After calculating the work geographic index in subparagraph (A)(iii), for purposes of payment for services furnished on or after , and before , the Secretary shall increase the work geographic index to 1.00 for any locality for which such work geographic index is less than 1.00.January 1, 2004January 1, 2027

children.heading: Floor for practice expense, malpractice, and work geographic indices for services furnished in Alaska3So in original. No subpar. (F) has been enacted.3
children.label: G Floor for practice expense, malpractice, and work geographic indices for services furnished in Alaska3So in original. No subpar. (F) has been enacted.3
children.text: For purposes of payment for services furnished in Alaska on or after , and before , after calculating the practice expense, malpractice, and work geographic indices in clauses (i), (ii), and (iii) of subparagraph (A) and in subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall increase any such index to 1.67 if such index would otherwise be less than 1.67. For purposes of payment for services furnished in the State described in the preceding sentence on or after , after calculating the work geographic index in subparagraph (A)(iii), the Secretary shall increase the work geographic index to 1.5 if such index would otherwise be less than 1.5January 1, 2004January 1, 2006January 1, 20091

children.heading: Practice expense geographic adjustment for 2010 and subsequent years
children.label: H Practice expense geographic adjustment for 2010 and subsequent years

children.heading: For 2010
children.label: i For 2010
children.text: Subject to clause (iii), for services furnished during 2010, the employee wage and rent portions of the practice expense geographic index described in subparagraph (A)(i) shall reflect ½ of the difference between the relative costs of employee wages and rents in each of the different fee schedule areas and the national average of such employee wages and rents.

children.heading: For 2011
children.label: ii For 2011
children.text: Subject to clause (iii), for services furnished during 2011, the employee wage and rent portions of the practice expense geographic index described in subparagraph (A)(i) shall reflect ½ of the difference between the relative costs of employee wages and rents in each of the different fee schedule areas and the national average of such employee wages and rents.

children.heading: Hold harmless
children.label: iii Hold harmless
children.text: The practice expense portion of the geographic adjustment factor applied in a fee schedule area for services furnished in 2010 or 2011 shall not, as a result of the application of clause (i) or (ii), be reduced below the practice expense portion of the geographic adjustment factor under subparagraph (A)(i) (as calculated prior to the application of such clause (i) or (ii), respectively) for such area for such year.

children.label: I
children.text: The feasibility of using actual data or reliable survey data developed by medical organizations on the costs of operating a medical practice, including office rents and non-physician staff wages, in different fee schedule areas.

children.label: II
children.text: The office expense portion of the practice expense geographic adjustment described in subparagraph (A)(i), including the extent to which types of office expenses are determined in local markets instead of national markets.

children.label: III
children.text: The weights assigned to each of the categories within the practice expense geographic adjustment described in subparagraph (A)(i).

children.heading: Revision for 2012 and subsequent years
children.label: v Revision for 2012 and subsequent years
I
children.text: basing the office rents component and its weight on office expenses that vary among fee schedule areas; and

children.label: II
children.text: considering a representative range of professional and non-professional personnel employed in a medical office based on the use of the American Community Survey data or other reliable data for wage adjustments.

children.heading: Floor for practice expense index for services furnished in frontier States
children.label: I Floor for practice expense index for services furnished in frontier States

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), for purposes of payment for services furnished in a frontier State (as defined in ) on or after , after calculating the practice expense index in subparagraph (A)(i), the Secretary shall increase any such index to 1.00 if such index would otherwise be less that 1.00. The preceding sentence shall not be applied in a budget neutral manner.section 1395ww(d)(3)(E)(iii)(II) of this title4January 1, 2011So in original. Probably should be “than”.4

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: This subparagraph shall not apply to services furnished in a State that receives a non-labor related share adjustment under .section 1395ww(d)(5)(H) of this title

children.heading: Computation of geographic adjustment factor
children.label: 2 Computation of geographic adjustment factor
children.text: For purposes of subsection (b)(1)(C), for all physicians’ services for each fee schedule area the Secretary shall establish a geographic adjustment factor equal to the sum of the geographic cost-of-practice adjustment factor (specified in paragraph (3)), the geographic malpractice adjustment factor (specified in paragraph (4)), and the geographic physician work adjustment factor (specified in paragraph (5)) for the service and the area.

children.label: A
children.text: the proportion of the total relative value for the service that reflects the relative value units for the practice expense component, and

children.label: B
children.text: the geographic cost-of-practice index value for the area for the service, based on the index established under paragraph (1)(A)(i) or (1)(B) (as the case may be).

children.label: A
children.text: the proportion of the total relative value for the service that reflects the relative value units for the malpractice component, and

children.label: B
children.text: the geographic malpractice index value for the area, based on the index established under paragraph (1)(A)(ii).

children.label: A
children.text: the proportion of the total relative value for the service that reflects the relative value units for the work component, and

children.label: B
children.text: the geographic physician work index value for the area, based on the index established under paragraph (1)(A)(iii).

children.heading: Use of MSAs as fee schedule areas in California
children.label: 6 Use of MSAs as fee schedule areas in California
i
children.text: Each Metropolitan Statistical Area (each in this paragraph referred to as an “MSA”), as defined by the Director of the Office of Management and Budget as of December 31 of the previous year, shall be a fee schedule area.

children.label: ii
children.text: All areas not included in an MSA shall be treated as a single rest-of-State fee schedule area.

children.heading: Transition for MSAs previously in rest-of-state payment locality or in locality 3
children.label: B Transition for MSAs previously in rest-of-state payment locality or in locality 3

children.heading: Current law component
children.label: I Current law component
children.text: The old weighting factor (described in clause (ii)) for such year multiplied by the geographic index values under this subsection for the fee schedule area that included such MSA that would have applied in such area (as estimated by the Secretary) if this paragraph did not apply.

children.heading: MSA-based component
children.label: II MSA-based component
children.text: The MSA-based weighting factor (described in clause (iii)) for such year multiplied by the geographic index values computed for the fee schedule area under subparagraph (A) for the year (determined without regard to this subparagraph).

children.label: I
children.text: for 2017, is ⅚; and

children.label: II
children.text: for each succeeding year, is the old weighting factor described in this clause for the previous year minus ⅙.

children.heading: MSA-based weighting factor
children.label: iii MSA-based weighting factor
children.text: The MSA-based weighting factor described in this clause for a year is 1 minus the old weighting factor under clause (ii) for that year.

children.heading: Hold harmless
children.label: C Hold harmless
children.text: For services furnished in a transition area in California during a year beginning with 2017, the geographic index values to be applied under this subsection for such year shall not be less than the corresponding geographic index values that would have applied in such transition area (as estimated by the Secretary) if this paragraph did not apply.

children.text: The rest-of-State payment locality.

children.heading: References to fee schedule areas
children.label: E References to fee schedule areas
children.text: Effective for services furnished on or after , for California, any reference in this section to a fee schedule area shall be deemed a reference to a fee schedule area established in accordance with this paragraph.January 1, 2017

children.heading: For 2001
children.label: A For 2001
children.text: For purposes of such calculations for 2001, the sustainable growth rates for fiscal year 2000 and the years 2000 and 2001 shall be determined on the basis of the best data available to the Secretary as of .September 1, 2000

children.heading: For 2002
children.label: B For 2002
children.text: For purposes of such calculations for 2002, the sustainable growth rates for fiscal year 2000 and for years 2000, 2001, and 2002 shall be determined on the basis of the best data available to the Secretary as of .September 1, 2001

children.text: the sustainable growth rates for that year and the preceding 2 years shall be determined on the basis of the best data available to the Secretary as of September 1 of the year preceding the year for which the calculation is made; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the sustainable growth rate for any year before a year described in clause (i) shall be the rate as most recently determined for that year under this subsection.

children.heading: Services included in physicians’ services
children.label: A Services included in physicians’ services
children.text: The term “physicians’ services” includes other items and services (such as clinical diagnostic laboratory tests and radiology services), specified by the Secretary, that are commonly performed or furnished by a physician or in a physician’s office, but does not include services furnished to a Medicare+ÐChoice plan enrollee.

children.heading: Medicare+Choice plan enrollee
children.label: B Medicare+Choice plan enrollee
children.text: The term “Medicare+Choice plan enrollee” means, with respect to a fiscal year, an individual enrolled under this part who has elected to receive benefits under this subchapter for the fiscal year through a Medicare+Choice plan offered under part C, and also includes an individual who is receiving benefits under this part through enrollment with an eligible organization with a risk-sharing contract under .section 1395mm of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a fiscal year, in the case of fiscal year 1998, fiscal year 1999, and fiscal year 2000; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a calendar year with respect to a year beginning with 2000;

children.heading: Application of limiting charge
children.label: i Application of limiting charge
children.text: No person may bill or collect an actual charge for the service in excess of the limiting charge described in paragraph (2) for such service.

children.heading: No liability for excess charges
children.label: ii No liability for excess charges
children.text: No person is liable for payment of any amounts billed for the service in excess of such limiting charge.

children.heading: Correction of excess charges
children.label: iii Correction of excess charges
children.text: If such a physician, supplier, or other person bills, but does not collect, an actual charge for a service in violation of clause (i), the physician, supplier, or other person shall reduce on a timely basis the actual charge billed for the service to an amount not to exceed the limiting charge for the service.

children.heading: Refund of excess collections
children.label: iv Refund of excess collections
children.text: If such a physician, supplier, or other person collects an actual charge for a service in violation of clause (i), the physician, supplier, or other person shall provide on a timely basis a refund to the individual charged in the amount by which the amount collected exceeded the limiting charge for the service. The amount of such a refund shall be reduced to the extent the individual has an outstanding balance owed by the individual to the physician.

children.label: i
children.text: knowingly and willfully bills or collects for services in violation of subparagraph (A)(i) on a repeated basis, or

children.label: ii
children.text: fails to comply with clause (iii) or (iv) of subparagraph (A) on a timely basis,

children.heading: Timely basis
children.label: C Timely basis
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, a correction of a bill for an excess charge or refund of an amount with respect to a violation of subparagraph (A)(i) in the case of a service is considered to be provided “on a timely basis”, if the reduction or refund is made not later than 30 days after the date the physician, supplier, or other person is notified by the carrier under this part of such violation and of the requirements of subparagraph (A).

children.label: i
children.text: the maximum allowable actual charge (as determined under as of , or, if less, the maximum actual charge otherwise permitted for the service under this part as of such date) for the service of the physician, exceedssection 1395u(j)(1)(C) of this titleDecember 31, 1990

children.label: ii
children.text: the recognized payment amount for the service of the physician (as a nonparticipating physician) as of such date.

children.label: i

children.label: ii
children.text: the recognized payment amount for the service for nonparticipating physicians as of such date.

children.heading: After 1992
children.label: C After 1992
children.text: For physicians’ services furnished in a year after 1992, the “limiting charge” shall be 115 percent of the recognized payment amount under this part for nonparticipating physicians or for nonparticipating suppliers or other persons.

children.heading: Recognized payment amount
children.label: D Recognized payment amount
children.text: In this section, the term “recognized payment amount” means, for services furnished on or after , the fee schedule amount determined under subsection (a) (or, if payment under this part is made on a basis other than the fee schedule under this section, 95 percent of the other payment basis), and, for services furnished during 1991, the applicable percentage (as defined in ) of the prevailing charge (or fee schedule amount) for nonparticipating physicians for that year.January 1, 1992section 1395u(b)(4)(A)(iv) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on charges for medicare beneficiaries eligible for medicaid benefits
children.label: 3 Limitation on charges for medicare beneficiaries eligible for medicaid benefits

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Payment for physicians’ services furnished on or after , to an individual who is enrolled under this part and eligible for any medical assistance (including as a qualified medicare beneficiary, as defined in ) with respect to such services under a State plan approved under subchapter XIX may only be made on an assignment-related basis and the provisions of apply to further limit permissible charges under this section.April 1, 1990section 1396d(p)(1) of this titlesection 1396a(n)(3)(A) of this title

children.heading: Penalty
children.label: B Penalty
children.text: A person may not bill for physicians’ services subject to subparagraph (A) other than on an assignment-related basis. No person is liable for payment of any amounts billed for such a service in violation of the previous sentence. If a person knowingly and willfully bills for physicians’ services in violation of the first sentence, the Secretary may apply sanctions against the person in accordance with .section 1395u(j)(2) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: shall complete and submit a claim for such service on a standard claim form specified by the Secretary to the carrier on behalf of a beneficiary, and

children.label: ii
children.text: may not impose any charge relating to completing and submitting such a form.

children.label: i
children.text: With respect to an assigned claim wherever a physician, provider, supplier or other person (or an employer or facility in the cases described in ) fails to submit such a claim as required in subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall reduce by 10 percent the amount that would otherwise be paid for such claim under this part.section 1395u(b)(6)(A) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: If a physician, supplier, or other person (or an employer or facility in the cases described in ) fails to submit a claim required to be submitted under subparagraph (A) or imposes a charge in violation of such subparagraph, the Secretary shall apply the sanction with respect to such a violation in the same manner as a sanction may be imposed under for a violation of .section 1395u(b)(6)(A) of this titlesection 1395u(p)(3) of this titlesection 1395u(p)(1) of this title

children.heading: Electronic billing; direct deposit
children.label: 5 Electronic billing; direct deposit
children.text: The Secretary shall encourage and develop a system providing for expedited payment for claims submitted electronically. The Secretary shall also encourage and provide incentives allowing for direct deposit as payments for services furnished by participating physicians. The Secretary shall provide physicians with such technical information as necessary to enable such physicians to submit claims electronically. The Secretary shall submit a plan to Congress on this paragraph by .May 1, 1990

children.label: i
children.text: the actual charges of nonparticipating physicians for physicians’ services furnished on or after , to individuals enrolled under this part, andJanuary 1, 1991

children.label: ii
children.text: changes (by specialty, type of service, and geographic area) in (I) the proportion of expenditures for physicians’ services provided under this part by participating physicians, (II) the proportion of expenditures for such services for which payment is made under this part on an assignment-related basis, and (III) the amounts charged above the recognized payment amounts under this part.

children.heading: Report
children.label: B Report
children.text: The Secretary shall, by not later than April 15 of each year (beginning in 1992), report to the Congress information on the extent to which actual charges exceed limiting charges, the number and types of services involved, and the average amount of excess charges and information regarding the changes described in subparagraph (A)(ii).

children.heading: Plan
children.label: C Plan
children.text: If the Secretary finds that there has been a significant decrease in the proportions described in subclauses (I) and (II) of subparagraph (A)(ii) or an increase in the amounts described in subclause (III) of that subparagraph, the Secretary shall develop a plan to address such a problem and transmit to Congress recommendations regarding the plan. The Medicare Payment Advisory Commission shall review the Secretary’s plan and recommendations and transmit to Congress its comments regarding such plan and recommendations.

children.label: i
children.text: changes in the utilization of and access to services furnished under this part within geographic, population, and service related categories,

children.label: ii
children.text: possible sources of inappropriate utilization of services furnished under this part which contribute to the overall level of expenditures under this part, and

children.label: iii
children.text: factors underlying these changes and their interrelationships.

children.heading: Report
children.label: B Report
children.text: The Secretary shall by not later than April 15, of each year (beginning with 1991) report to the Congress on the changes described in subparagraph (A)(i) and shall include in the report an examination of the factors (including factors relating to different services and specific categories and groups of services and geographic and demographic variations in utilization) which may contribute to such changes.5So in original. The comma probably should not appear.5

children.label: i
children.text: addressing any identified patterns of inappropriate utilization,

children.label: ii
children.text: on utilization review,

children.label: iii
children.text: on physician education or patient education,

children.label: iv
children.text: addressing any problems of beneficiary access to care made evident by the monitoring process, and

children.label: v
children.text: on such other matters as the Secretary deems appropriate.

children.heading: Sending information to physicians
children.label: h Sending information to physicians
children.text: Before the beginning of each year (beginning with 1992), the Secretary shall send to each physician or nonparticipating supplier or other person furnishing physicians’ services (as defined in subsection (j)(3)) furnishing physicians’ services under this part, for services commonly performed by the physician, supplier, or other person, information on fee schedule amounts that apply for the year in the fee schedule area for participating and non-participating physicians, and the maximum amount that may be charged consistent with subsection (g)(2). Such information shall be transmitted in conjunction with notices to physicians, suppliers, and other persons under (relating to the participating physician program) for a year.section 1395u(h) of this title

children.heading: Restriction on administrative and judicial review
children.label: 1 Restriction on administrative and judicial review
A
children.text: the determination of the adjusted historical payment basis (as defined in subsection (a)(2)(D)(i)),

children.label: B
children.text: the determination of relative values and relative value units under subsection (c), including adjustments under subsections (c)(2)(F), (c)(2)(H), and (c)(2)(I) and section 13515(b) of the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1993,

children.label: C
children.text: the determination of conversion factors under subsection (d), including without limitation a prospective redetermination of the sustainable growth rates for any or all previous fiscal years,

children.label: D
children.text: the establishment of geographic adjustment factors under subsection (e),

children.label: E
children.text: the establishment of the system for the coding of physicians’ services under this section, and

children.label: F
children.text: the collection and use of information in the determination of relative values under subsection (c)(2)(M).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), in the case of a surgical service furnished by a physician, if payment is made separately under this part for the services of a physician serving as an assistant-at-surgery, the fee schedule amount shall not exceed 16 percent of the fee schedule amount otherwise determined under this section for the global surgical service involved.

children.heading: Denial of payment in certain cases
children.label: B Denial of payment in certain cases
children.text: If the Secretary determines, based on the most recent data available, that for a surgical procedure (or class of surgical procedures) the national average percentage of such procedure performed under this part which involve the use of a physician as an assistant at surgery is less than 5 percent, no payment may be made under this part for services of an assistant at surgery involved in the procedure.

children.label: A
children.text: a carrier may not make any adjustment in the payment amount under on the basis that the payment amount is higher than the charge applicable, for comparable services and under comparable circumstances, to the policyholders and subscribers of the carrier,section 1395u(b)(3)(B) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: no payment adjustment may be made under , andsection 1395u(b)(8) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: shall not apply.section 1395u(b)(9) of this title

children.heading: Category
children.label: 1 Category
children.text: For services furnished before , the term “category” means, with respect to physicians’ services, surgical services, and all physicians’ services other than surgical services (as defined by the Secretary and including anesthesia services), primary care services (as defined in ), and all other physicians’ services. The Secretary shall define surgical services and publish such definition in the Federal Register no later than , after consultation with organizations representing physicians.January 1, 1998May 1, 1990section 1395u(i)(4) of this title

children.heading: Fee schedule area
children.label: 2 Fee schedule area
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (e)(6)(D), the term “fee schedule area” means a locality used under for purposes of computing payment amounts for physicians’ services.section 1395u(b) of this title

children.heading: Physicians’ services
children.label: 3 Physicians’ services
children.text: The term “physicians’ services” includes items and services described in paragraphs (1), (2)(A), (2)(D), (2)(G), (2)(P) (with respect to services described in subparagraphs (A) and (C) of section 1395x()(2) of this title), (2)(R) (with respect to services described in subparagraphs (B), (C), and (D) of ), (2)(S), (2)(W), (2)(AA), (2)(DD), (2)(EE), (2)(FF) (including administration of the health risk assessment), (3), (4), (13), (14) (with respect to services described in ), and (15) of (other than clinical diagnostic laboratory tests and, except for purposes of subsections (a)(3), (g), and (h) such other items and services as the Secretary may specify).oosection 1395x(pp)(1) of this titlesection 1395x(nn)(2) of this titlesection 1395x(s) of this title6So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.6

children.heading: Practice expenses
children.label: 4 Practice expenses
children.text: The term “practice expenses” includes all expenses for furnishing physicians’ services, excluding malpractice expenses, physician compensation, and other physician fringe benefits.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall implement a system for the reporting by eligible professionals of data on quality measures specified under paragraph (2). Such data shall be submitted in a form and manner specified by the Secretary (by program instruction or otherwise), which may include submission of such data on claims under this part.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of applying this subsection for the reporting of data on quality measures for covered professional services furnished during the period beginning , and ending , the quality measures specified under this paragraph are the measures identified as 2007 physician quality measures under the Physician Voluntary Reporting Program as published on the public website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services as of , except as may be changed by the Secretary based on the results of a consensus-based process in January of 2007, if such change is published on such website by not later than .July 1, 2007December 31, 2007December 20, 2006April 1, 2007

children.heading: Subsequent refinements in application permitted
children.label: ii Subsequent refinements in application permitted
children.text: The Secretary may, from time to time (but not later than ), publish on such website (without notice or opportunity for public comment) modifications or refinements (such as code additions, corrections, or revisions) for the application of quality measures previously published under clause (i), but may not, under this clause, change the quality measures under the reporting system.July 1, 2007

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: iii Implementation
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary may implement by program instruction or otherwise this subsection for 2007.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of reporting data on quality measures for covered professional services furnished during 2008 and 2009, the quality measures specified under this paragraph for covered professional services shall be measures that have been adopted or endorsed by a consensus organization (such as the National Quality Forum or AQA), that include measures that have been submitted by a physician specialty, and that the Secretary identifies as having used a consensus-based process for developing such measures. Such measures shall include structural measures, such as the use of electronic health records and electronic prescribing technology.

children.heading: Proposed set of measures
children.label: ii Proposed set of measures
children.text: Not later than August 15 of each of 2007 and 2008, the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register a proposed set of quality measures that the Secretary determines are described in clause (i) and would be appropriate for eligible professionals to use to submit data to the Secretary in 2008 or 2009, as applicable. The Secretary shall provide for a period of public comment on such set of measures.

children.heading: Final set of measures
children.label: iii Final set of measures
children.text: Not later than November 15 of each of 2007 and 2008, the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register a final set of quality measures that the Secretary determines are described in clause (i) and would be appropriate for eligible professionals to use to submit data to the Secretary in 2008 or 2009, as applicable.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), for purposes of reporting data on quality measures for covered professional services furnished during 2010 and each subsequent year, subject to subsection (m)(3)(C), the quality measures (including electronic prescribing quality measures) specified under this paragraph shall be such measures selected by the Secretary from measures that have been endorsed by the entity with a contract with the Secretary under .section 1395aaa(a) of this title

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: In the case of a specified area or medical topic determined appropriate by the Secretary for which a feasible and practical measure has not been endorsed by the entity with a contract under , the Secretary may specify a measure that is not so endorsed as long as due consideration is given to measures that have been endorsed or adopted by a consensus organization identified by the Secretary, such as the AQA alliance.section 1395aaa(a) of this title

children.heading: Opportunity to provide input on measures for 2009 and subsequent years
children.label: D Opportunity to provide input on measures for 2009 and subsequent years
children.text: For each quality measure (including an electronic prescribing quality measure) adopted by the Secretary under subparagraph (B) (with respect to 2009) or subparagraph (C), the Secretary shall ensure that eligible professionals have the opportunity to provide input during the development, endorsement, or selection of measures applicable to services they furnish.

children.heading: Covered professional services and eligible professionals defined
children.label: 3 Covered professional services and eligible professionals defined

children.heading: Covered professional services
children.label: A Covered professional services
children.text: The term “covered professional services” means services for which payment is made under, or is based on, the fee schedule established under this section and which are furnished by an eligible professional.

children.label: i
children.text: A physician.

children.label: ii
children.text: A practitioner described in .section 1395u(b)(18)(C) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: A physical or occupational therapist or a qualified speech-language pathologist.

children.label: iv
children.text: Beginning with 2009, a qualified audiologist (as defined in section 1395x()(3)(B) of this title).ll

children.heading: Use of registry-based reporting
children.label: 4 Use of registry-based reporting
children.text: As part of the publication of proposed and final quality measures for 2008 under clauses (ii) and (iii) of paragraph (2)(B), the Secretary shall address a mechanism whereby an eligible professional may provide data on quality measures through an appropriate medical registry (such as the Society of Thoracic Surgeons National Database) or through a Maintenance of Certification program operated by a specialty body of the American Board of Medical Specialties that meets the criteria for such a registry, as identified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Identification units
children.label: 5 Identification units
children.text: For purposes of applying this subsection, the Secretary may identify eligible professionals through billing units, which may include the use of the Provider Identification Number, the unique physician identification number (described in section 1395(q)(1) of this title), the taxpayer identification number, or the National Provider Identifier. For purposes of applying this subsection for 2007, the Secretary shall use the taxpayer identification number as the billing unit.l

children.heading: Education and outreach
children.label: 6 Education and outreach
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for education and outreach to eligible professionals on the operation of this subsection.

children.heading: Limitations on review
children.label: 7 Limitations on review
children.text: There shall be no administrative or judicial review under , section 1395 of this title, or otherwise, of the development and implementation of the reporting system under paragraph (1), including identification of quality measures under paragraph (2) and the application of paragraphs (4) and (5).section 1395ff of this titleoo

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 8 Implementation
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out this subsection acting through the Administrator of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services.

children.heading: Continued application for purposes of MIPS and for certain professionals volunteering to report
children.label: 9 Continued application for purposes of MIPS and for certain professionals volunteering to report
A
children.text: for purposes of subsection (q); and

children.label: B
children.text: for eligible professionals who are not MIPS eligible professionals (as defined in subsection (q)(1)(C)) for the year involved.

children.heading: Physician Assistance and Quality Initiative Fund
children.label: l Physician Assistance and Quality Initiative Fund

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish under this subsection a Physician Assistance and Quality Initiative Fund (in this subsection referred to as the “Fund”) which shall be available to the Secretary for physician payment and quality improvement initiatives, which may include application of an adjustment to the update of the conversion factor under subsection (d).

children.label: I
children.text: For expenditures during 2008, an amount equal to $150,500,000.

children.label: II
children.text: For expenditures during 2009, an amount equal to $24,500,000.

children.label: I 2008
children.text: The amount available for expenditures during 2008 shall be reduced as provided by subparagraph (A) of section 225(c)(1) and section 524 of the Departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2008 (division G of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2008).

children.label: II 2009
children.text: The amount available for expenditures during 2009 shall be reduced as provided by subparagraph (B) of such section 225(c)(1).

children.heading: Timely obligation of all available funds for services
children.label: B Timely obligation of all available funds for services
i
children.text: 2008 for payment with respect to physicians’ services furnished during 2008; and

children.label: ii
children.text: 2009 for payment with respect to physicians’ services furnished during 2009.

children.heading: Payment from Trust Fund
children.label: C Payment from Trust Fund
children.text: The amount specified in subparagraph (A) shall be available to the Fund, as expenditures are made from the Fund, from the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under .section 1395t of this title

children.heading: Funding limitation
children.label: D Funding limitation
children.text: Amounts in the Fund shall be available in advance of appropriations in accordance with subparagraph (B) but only if the total amount obligated from the Fund does not exceed the amount available to the Fund under subparagraph (A). The Secretary may obligate funds from the Fund only if the Secretary determines (and the Chief Actuary of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services and the appropriate budget officer certify) that there are available in the Fund sufficient amounts to cover all such obligations incurred consistent with the previous sentence.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: E Construction
children.text: In the case that expenditures from the Fund are applied to, or otherwise affect, a conversion factor under subsection (d) for a year, the conversion factor under such subsection shall be computed for a subsequent year as if such application or effect had never occurred.

children.heading: Incentive payments for quality reporting
children.label: m Incentive payments for quality reporting
i
children.text: there are any quality measures that have been established under the physician reporting system that are applicable to any such services furnished by such professional for such reporting period; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the eligible professional satisfactorily submits (as determined under this subsection) to the Secretary data on such quality measures in accordance with such reporting system for such reporting period,

children.label: i
children.text: for 2007 and 2008, 1.5 percent;

children.label: ii
children.text: for 2009 and 2010, 2.0 percent;

children.label: iii
children.text: for 2011, 1.0 percent; and

children.label: iv
children.text: for 2012, 2013, and 2014, 0.5 percent.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (D), for 2009 through 2013, with respect to covered professional services furnished during a reporting period by an eligible professional, if the eligible professional is a successful electronic prescriber for such reporting period, in addition to the amount otherwise paid under this part, there also shall be paid to the eligible professional (or to an employer or facility in the cases described in clause (A) of ) or, in the case of a group practice under paragraph (3)(C), to the group practice, from the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund established under an amount equal to the applicable electronic prescribing percent of the Secretary’s estimate (based on claims submitted not later than 2 months after the end of the reporting period) of the allowed charges under this part for all such covered professional services furnished by the eligible professional (or, in the case of a group practice under paragraph (3)(C), by the group practice) during the reporting period.section 1395u(b)(6) of this titlesection 1395t of this title

children.heading: Limitation with respect to electronic prescribing quality measures
children.label: B Limitation with respect to electronic prescribing quality measures
i
children.text: the allowed charges under this part for all covered professional services furnished by the eligible professional (or group, as applicable) for the codes to which the electronic prescribing quality measure applies (as identified by the Secretary and published on the Internet website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services as of , and as subsequently modified by the Secretary) are less than 10 percent of the total of the allowed charges under this part for all such covered professional services furnished by the eligible professional (or the group, as applicable); orJanuary 1, 2008

children.label: ii
children.text: if determined appropriate by the Secretary, the eligible professional does not submit (including both electronically and nonelectronically) a sufficient number (as determined by the Secretary) of prescriptions under part D.

children.label: i
children.text: for 2009 and 2010, 2.0 percent;

children.label: ii
children.text: for 2011 and 2012, 1.0 percent; and

children.label: iii
children.text: for 2013, 0.5 percent.

children.heading: Limitation with respect to EHR incentive payments
children.label: D Limitation with respect to EHR incentive payments
children.text: The provisions of this paragraph shall not apply to an eligible professional (or, in the case of a group practice under paragraph (3)(C), to the group practice) if, for the EHR reporting period the eligible professional (or group practice) receives an incentive payment under subsection ()(1)(A) with respect to a certified EHR technology (as defined in subsection ()(4)) that has the capability of electronic prescribing.oo

children.heading: Satisfactory reporting and successful electronic prescriber described
children.label: 3 Satisfactory reporting and successful electronic prescriber described

children.heading: Three or fewer quality measures applicable
children.label: i Three or fewer quality measures applicable
children.text: If there are no more than 3 quality measures that are provided under the physician reporting system and that are applicable to such services of such professional furnished during the period, each such quality measure has been reported under such system in at least 80 percent of the cases in which such measure is reportable under the system.

children.heading: Four or more quality measures applicable
children.label: ii Four or more quality measures applicable
children.text: If there are 4 or more quality measures that are provided under the physician reporting system and that are applicable to such services of such professional furnished during the period, at least 3 such quality measures have been reported under such system in at least 80 percent of the cases in which the respective measure is reportable under the system.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (2) and subsection (a)(5), an eligible professional shall be treated as a successful electronic prescriber for a reporting period (or, for purposes of subsection (a)(5), for the reporting period for a year) if the eligible professional meets the requirement described in clause (ii), or, if the Secretary determines appropriate, the requirement described in clause (iii). If the Secretary makes the determination under the preceding sentence to apply the requirement described in clause (iii) for a period, then the requirement described in clause (ii) shall not apply for such period.

children.heading: Requirement for submitting data on electronic prescribing quality measures
children.label: ii Requirement for submitting data on electronic prescribing quality measures
children.text: The requirement described in this clause is that, with respect to covered professional services furnished by an eligible professional during a reporting period (or, for purposes of subsection (a)(5), for the reporting period for a year), if there are any electronic prescribing quality measures that have been established under the physician reporting system and are applicable to any such services furnished by such professional for the period, such professional reported each such measure under such system in at least 50 percent of the cases in which such measure is reportable by such professional under such system.

children.heading: Requirement for electronically prescribing under part D
children.label: iii Requirement for electronically prescribing under part D
children.text: The requirement described in this clause is that the eligible professional electronically submitted a sufficient number (as determined by the Secretary) of prescriptions under part D during the reporting period (or, for purposes of subsection (a)(5), for the reporting period for a year).

children.heading: Use of part D data
children.label: iv Use of part D data
children.text: Notwithstanding sections 1395w–115(d)(2)(B) and 1395w–115(f)(2) of this title, the Secretary may use data regarding drug claims submitted for purposes of that are necessary for purposes of clause (iii), paragraph (2)(B)(ii), and paragraph (5)(G).section 1395w–115 of this title

children.heading: Standards for electronic prescribing
children.label: v Standards for electronic prescribing
children.text: To the extent practicable, in determining whether eligible professionals meet the requirements under clauses (ii) and (iii) for purposes of clause (i), the Secretary shall ensure that eligible professionals utilize electronic prescribing systems in compliance with standards established for such systems pursuant to the Part D Electronic Prescribing Program under .section 1395w–104(e) of this title

children.heading: Satisfactory reporting measures for group practices
children.label: C Satisfactory reporting measures for group practices

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: By , the Secretary shall establish and have in place a process under which eligible professionals in a group practice (as defined by the Secretary) shall be treated as satisfactorily submitting data on quality measures under subparagraph (A) and as meeting the requirement described in subparagraph (B)(ii) for covered professional services for a reporting period (or, for purposes of subsection (a)(5), for a reporting period for a year), or, for purposes of subsection (a)(8), for a quality reporting period for the year if, in lieu of reporting measures under subsection (k)(2)(C), the group practice reports measures determined appropriate by the Secretary, such as measures that target high-cost chronic conditions and preventive care, in a form and manner, and at a time, specified by the Secretary.January 1, 2010

children.heading: Statistical sampling model
children.label: ii Statistical sampling model
children.text: The process under clause (i) shall provide and, for 2016 and subsequent years, may provide for the use of a statistical sampling model to submit data on measures, such as the model used under the Physician Group Practice demonstration project under .section 1395cc–1 of this title

children.heading: No double payments
children.label: iii No double payments
children.text: Payments to a group practice under this subsection by reason of the process under clause (i) shall be in lieu of the payments that would otherwise be made under this subsection to eligible professionals in the group practice for satisfactorily submitting data on quality measures.

children.heading: Satisfactory reporting measures through participation in a qualified clinical data registry
children.label: D Satisfactory reporting measures through participation in a qualified clinical data registry
children.text: For 2014 and subsequent years, the Secretary shall treat an eligible professional as satisfactorily submitting data on quality measures under subparagraph (A) and, for 2016 and subsequent years, subparagraph (A) or (C) if, in lieu of reporting measures under subsection (k)(2)(C), the eligible professional is satisfactorily participating, as determined by the Secretary, in a qualified clinical data registry (as described in subparagraph (E)) for the year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish requirements for an entity to be considered a qualified clinical data registry.
