Such requirements shall include a requirement that the entity provide the Secretary with such information, at such times, and in such manner, as the Secretary determines necessary to carry out this subsection.

children.label: I
children.text: has in place mechanisms for the transparency of data elements and specifications, risk models, and measures;

children.label: II
children.text: requires the submission of data from participants with respect to multiple payers;

children.label: III
children.text: provides timely performance reports to participants at the individual participant level; and

children.label: IV
children.text: supports quality improvement initiatives for participants.

children.label: I
children.text: sections 1395aaa(b)(7) and 1395aaa–1(a) of this title shall not apply; and

children.label: II
children.text: measures endorsed by the entity with a contract with the Secretary under may be used.section 1395aaa(a) of this title

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: iv Consultation
children.text: In carrying out this subparagraph, the Secretary shall consult with interested parties.

children.label: I
children.text: A determination by the Secretary.

children.label: II
children.text: A designation by the Secretary of one or more independent organizations to make such determination.

children.heading: Authority to revise satisfactorily reporting data
children.label: F Authority to revise satisfactorily reporting data
children.text: For years after 2009, the Secretary, in consultation with stakeholders and experts, may revise the criteria under this subsection for satisfactorily submitting data on quality measures under subparagraph (A) and the criteria for submitting data on electronic prescribing quality measures under subparagraph (B)(ii).

children.heading: Form of payment
children.label: 4 Form of payment
children.text: The payment under this subsection shall be in the form of a single consolidated payment.

children.heading: Physician reporting system rules
children.label: A Physician reporting system rules
children.text: Paragraphs (5), (6), and (8) of subsection (k) shall apply for purposes of this subsection in the same manner as they apply for purposes of such subsection.

children.heading: Coordination with other bonus payments
children.label: B Coordination with other bonus payments
children.text: The provisions of this subsection shall not be taken into account in applying subsections (m) and (u) of section 1395 of this title and any payment under such subsections shall not be taken into account in computing allowable charges under this subsection.l

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: C Implementation
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, for 2007, 2008, and 2009, the Secretary may implement by program instruction or otherwise this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to the succeeding provisions of this subparagraph, for purposes of determining whether a measure is applicable to the covered professional services of an eligible professional under this subsection for 2007 and 2008, the Secretary shall presume that if an eligible professional submits data for a measure, such measure is applicable to such professional.

children.heading: Method
children.label: ii Method
children.text: The Secretary may establish procedures to validate (by sampling or other means as the Secretary determines to be appropriate) whether measures applicable to covered professional services of an eligible professional have been reported.

children.heading: Denial of payment authority
children.label: iii Denial of payment authority
children.text: If the Secretary determines that an eligible professional (or, in the case of a group practice under paragraph (3)(C), the group practice) has not reported measures applicable to covered professional services of such professional, the Secretary shall not pay the incentive payment under this subsection. If such payments for such period have already been made, the Secretary shall recoup such payments from the eligible professional (or the group practice).

children.label: i
children.text: the determination of measures applicable to services furnished by eligible professionals under this subsection;

children.label: ii
children.text: the determination of satisfactory reporting under this subsection;

children.label: iii
children.text: the determination of a successful electronic prescriber under paragraph (3), the limitation under paragraph (2)(B), and the exception under subsection (a)(5)(B); and

children.label: iv
children.text: the determination of any incentive payment under this subsection and the payment adjustment under paragraphs (5)(A) and (8)(A) of subsection (a).

children.heading: Extension
children.label: F Extension
children.text: For 2008 through reporting periods occurring in 2015, the Secretary shall establish and, for reporting periods occurring in 2016 and subsequent years, the Secretary may establish alternative criteria for satisfactorily reporting under this subsection and alternative reporting periods under paragraph (6)(C) for reporting groups of measures under subsection (k)(2)(B) and for reporting using the method specified in subsection (k)(4).

children.label: i
children.text: The eligible professionals (or, in the case of reporting under paragraph (3)(C), the group practices) who satisfactorily submitted data on quality measures under this subsection.

children.label: ii
children.text: The eligible professionals (or, in the case of reporting under paragraph (3)(C), the group practices) who are successful electronic prescribers.

children.heading: Feedback
children.label: H Feedback
children.text: The Secretary shall provide timely feedback to eligible professionals on the performance of the eligible professional with respect to satisfactorily submitting data on quality measures under this subsection.

children.heading: Informal appeals process
children.label: I Informal appeals process
children.text: The Secretary shall, by not later than , establish and have in place an informal process for eligible professionals to seek a review of the determination that an eligible professional did not satisfactorily submit data on quality measures under this subsection.January 1, 2011

children.heading: Eligible professional; covered professional services
children.label: A Eligible professional; covered professional services
children.text: The terms “eligible professional” and “covered professional services” have the meanings given such terms in subsection (k)(3).

children.heading: Physician reporting system
children.label: B Physician reporting system
children.text: The term “physician reporting system” means the system established under subsection (k).

children.label: I
children.text: for 2007, the period beginning on , and ending on ; andJuly 1, 2007December 31, 2007

children.label: II
children.text: for 2008 and subsequent years, the entire year.

children.heading: Authority to revise reporting period
children.label: ii Authority to revise reporting period
children.text: For years after 2009, the Secretary may revise the reporting period under clause (i) if the Secretary determines such revision is appropriate, produces valid results on measures reported, and is consistent with the goals of maximizing scientific validity and reducing administrative burden. If the Secretary revises such period pursuant to the preceding sentence, the term “reporting period” shall mean such revised period.

children.heading: Reference
children.label: iii Reference
children.text: Any reference in this subsection to a reporting period with respect to the application of subsection (a)(5) (a)(8) shall be deemed a reference to the reporting period under subsection (a)(5)(D)(iii) or the quality reporting period under subsection (a)(8)(D)(iii), respectively.78So in original.7So in original. Probably should be “(a)(8)(C)(iii),”.8

children.heading: Integration of physician quality reporting and EHR reporting
children.label: 7 Integration of physician quality reporting and EHR reporting
i
children.text: meaningful use of an electronic health record for purposes of subsection (); ando

children.label: ii
children.text: quality of care furnished to an individual.

children.label: B
children.text: Such other activities as specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For 2011 through 2014, if an eligible professional meets the requirements described in subparagraph (B), the applicable quality percent for such year, as described in clauses (iii) and (iv) of paragraph (1)(B), shall be increased by 0.5 percentage points.

children.label: I
children.text: satisfactorily submit data on quality measures for purposes of paragraph (1) for a year; and

children.label: aa
children.text: the criteria for a registry (as described in subsection (k)(4)); or

children.label: bb
children.text: an alternative form and manner determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: participates in such a Maintenance of Certification program for a year; and

children.label: II
children.text: successfully completes a qualified Maintenance of Certification Program practice assessment (as defined in subparagraph (C)(ii)) for such year.

children.label: I
children.text: in a form and manner specified by the Secretary, that the eligible professional has successfully met the requirements of clause (ii) (which may be in the form of a structural measure);

children.label: II
children.text: if requested by the Secretary, on the survey of patient experience with care (as described in subparagraph (C)(ii)(II)); and

children.label: III
children.text: as the Secretary may require, on the methods, measures, and data used under the Maintenance of Certification Program and the qualified Maintenance of Certification Program practice assessment.

children.label: I
children.text: The program requires the physician to maintain a valid, unrestricted medical license in the United States.

children.label: II
children.text: The program requires a physician to participate in educational and self-assessment programs that require an assessment of what was learned.

children.label: III
children.text: The program requires a physician to demonstrate, through a formalized, secure examination, that the physician has the fundamental diagnostic skills, medical knowledge, and clinical judgment to provide quality care in their respective specialty.

children.label: IV
children.text: The program requires successful completion of a qualified Maintenance of Certification Program practice assessment as described in clause (ii).

children.label: I
children.text: includes an initial assessment of an eligible professional’s practice that is designed to demonstrate the physician’s use of evidence-based medicine;

children.label: II
children.text: includes a survey of patient experience with care; and

children.label: III
children.text: requires a physician to implement a quality improvement intervention to address a practice weakness identified in the initial assessment under subclause (I) and then to remeasure to assess performance improvement after such intervention.

children.heading: Continued application for purposes of MIPS and for certain professionals volunteering to report
children.label: 9 Continued application for purposes of MIPS and for certain professionals volunteering to report
A
children.text: for purposes of subsection (q); and

children.label: B
children.text: for eligible professionals who are not MIPS eligible professionals (as defined in subsection (q)(1)(C)) for the year involved.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: i Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a Physician Feedback Program (in this subsection referred to as the “Program”).

children.heading: Reports on resources
children.label: ii Reports on resources
children.text: The Secretary shall use claims data under this subchapter (and may use other data) to provide confidential reports to physicians (and, as determined appropriate by the Secretary, to groups of physicians) that measure the resources involved in furnishing care to individuals under this subchapter.

children.heading: Inclusion of certain information
children.label: iii Inclusion of certain information
children.text: If determined appropriate by the Secretary, the Secretary may include information on the quality of care furnished to individuals under this subchapter by the physician (or group of physicians) in such reports.

children.label: i
children.text: on an episode basis;

children.label: ii
children.text: on a per capita basis; or

children.label: iii
children.text: on both an episode and a per capita basis.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 2 Implementation
children.text: The Secretary shall implement the Program by not later than .January 1, 2009

children.heading: Data for reports
children.label: 3 Data for reports
children.text: To the extent practicable, reports under the Program shall be based on the most recent data available.

children.label: A
children.text: physician specialties that account for a certain percentage of all spending for physicians’ services under this subchapter;

children.label: B
children.text: physicians who treat conditions that have a high cost or a high volume, or both, under this subchapter;

children.label: C
children.text: physicians who use a high amount of resources compared to other physicians;

children.label: D
children.text: physicians practicing in certain geographic areas; or

children.label: E
children.text: physicians who treat a minimum number of individuals under this subchapter.

children.heading: Authority to exclude certain information if insufficient information
children.label: 5 Authority to exclude certain information if insufficient information
children.text: The Secretary may exclude certain information regarding a service from a report under the Program with respect to a physician (or group of physicians) if the Secretary determines that there is insufficient information relating to that service to provide a valid report on that service.

children.heading: Adjustment of data
children.label: 6 Adjustment of data
children.text: To the extent practicable, the Secretary shall make appropriate adjustments to the data used in preparing reports under the Program, such as adjustments to take into account variations in health status and other patient characteristics. For adjustments for reports on utilization under paragraph (9), see subparagraph (D) of such paragraph.

children.heading: Education and outreach
children.label: 7 Education and outreach
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for education and outreach activities to physicians on the operation of, and methodologies employed under, the Program.

children.heading: Disclosure exemption
children.label: 8 Disclosure exemption
children.text: Reports under the Program shall be exempt from disclosure under .section 552 of title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall develop an episode grouper that combines separate but clinically related items and services into an episode of care for an individual, as appropriate.

children.heading: Timeline for development
children.label: ii Timeline for development
children.text: The episode grouper described in subparagraph (A) shall be developed by not later than .9So in original. Probably means cl. (i) of this subpar.9January 1, 2012

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: iii Public availability
children.text: The Secretary shall make the details of the episode grouper described in subparagraph (A) available to the public.9

children.heading: Endorsement
children.label: iv Endorsement
children.text: The Secretary shall seek endorsement of the episode grouper described in subparagraph (A) by the entity with a contract under .9section 1395aaa(a) of this title

children.heading: Reports on utilization
children.label: B Reports on utilization
children.text: Effective beginning with 2012, the Secretary shall provide reports to physicians that compare, as determined appropriate by the Secretary, patterns of resource use of the individual physician to such patterns of other physicians.

children.label: i
children.text: attribute episodes of care, in whole or in part, to physicians;

children.label: ii
children.text: identify appropriate physicians for purposes of comparison under subparagraph (B); and

children.label: iii
children.text: aggregate episodes of care attributed to a physician under clause (i) into a composite measure per individual.

children.label: i
children.text: to account for differences in socioeconomic and demographic characteristics, ethnicity, and health status of individuals (such as to recognize that less healthy individuals may require more intensive interventions); and

children.label: ii
children.text: to eliminate the effect of geographic adjustments in payment rates (as described in subsection (e)).

children.label: i
children.text: the methodologies established under subparagraph (C);

children.label: ii
children.text: information regarding any adjustments made to data under subparagraph (D); and

children.label: iii
children.text: aggregate reports with respect to physicians.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “physician” has the meaning given that term in .section 1395x(r)(1) of this title

children.heading: Treatment of groups
children.label: ii Treatment of groups
children.text: Such term includes, as the Secretary determines appropriate, a group of physicians.

children.heading: Limitations on review
children.label: G Limitations on review
children.text: There shall be no administrative or judicial review under , section 1395 of this title, or otherwise of the establishment of the methodology under subparagraph (C), including the determination of an episode of care under such methodology.section 1395ff of this titleoo

children.heading: Coordination with other value-based purchasing reforms
children.label: 10 Coordination with other value-based purchasing reforms
children.text: The Secretary shall coordinate the Program with the value-based payment modifier established under subsection (p) and, as the Secretary determines appropriate, other similar provisions of this subchapter.

children.heading: Reports ending with 2017
children.label: 11 Reports ending with 2017
children.text: Reports under the Program shall not be provided after . See subsection (q)(12) for reports under the eligible professionals Merit-based Incentive Payment System.December 31, 2017

children.heading: Incentives for adoption and meaningful use of certified EHR technology
children.label: o Incentives for adoption and meaningful use of certified EHR technology

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to the succeeding subparagraphs of this paragraph, with respect to covered professional services furnished by an eligible professional during a payment year (as defined in subparagraph (E)), if the eligible professional is a meaningful EHR user (as determined under paragraph (2)) for the EHR reporting period with respect to such year, in addition to the amount otherwise paid under this part, there also shall be paid to the eligible professional (or to an employer or facility in the cases described in clause (A) of ), from the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund established under an amount equal to 75 percent of the Secretary’s estimate (based on claims submitted not later than 2 months after the end of the payment year) of the allowed charges under this part for all such covered professional services furnished by the eligible professional during such year.section 1395u(b)(6) of this titlesection 1395t of this title

children.heading: No incentive payments with respect to years after 2016
children.label: ii No incentive payments with respect to years after 2016
children.text: No incentive payments may be made under this subsection with respect to a year after 2016.

children.heading: Limitations on amounts of incentive payments
children.label: B Limitations on amounts of incentive payments

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In no case shall the amount of the incentive payment provided under this paragraph for an eligible professional for a payment year exceed the applicable amount specified under this subparagraph with respect to such eligible professional and such year.

children.label: I
children.text: For the first payment year for such professional, $15,000 (or, if the first payment year for such eligible professional is 2011 or 2012, $18,000).

children.label: II
children.text: For the second payment year for such professional, $12,000.

children.label: III
children.text: For the third payment year for such professional, $8,000.

children.label: IV
children.text: For the fourth payment year for such professional, $4,000.

children.label: V
children.text: For the fifth payment year for such professional, $2,000.

children.label: VI
children.text: For any succeeding payment year for such professional, $0.

children.heading: Phase down for eligible professionals first adopting EHR after 2013
children.label: iii Phase down for eligible professionals first adopting EHR after 2013
children.text: If the first payment year for an eligible professional is after 2013, then the amount specified in this subparagraph for a payment year for such professional is the same as the amount specified in clause (ii) for such payment year for an eligible professional whose first payment year is 2013.

children.heading: Increase for certain eligible professionals
children.label: iv Increase for certain eligible professionals
children.text: In the case of an eligible professional who predominantly furnishes services under this part in an area that is designated by the Secretary (under ) as a health professional shortage area, the amount that would otherwise apply for a payment year for such professional under subclauses (I) through (V) of clause (ii) shall be increased by 10 percent. In implementing the preceding sentence, the Secretary may, as determined appropriate, apply provisions of subsections (m) and (u) of section 1395 of this title in a similar manner as such provisions apply under such subsection.section 254e(a)(1)(A) of this titlel

children.heading: No incentive payment if first adopting after 2014
children.label: v No incentive payment if first adopting after 2014
children.text: If the first payment year for an eligible professional is after 2014 then the applicable amount specified in this subparagraph for such professional for such year and any subsequent year shall be $0.

children.heading: Non-application to hospital-based eligible professionals
children.label: C Non-application to hospital-based eligible professionals

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: No incentive payment may be made under this paragraph in the case of a hospital-based eligible professional.

children.heading: Hospital-based eligible professional
children.label: ii Hospital-based eligible professional
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the term “hospital-based eligible professional” means, with respect to covered professional services furnished by an eligible professional during the EHR reporting period for a payment year, an eligible professional, such as a pathologist, anesthesiologist, or emergency physician, who furnishes substantially all of such services in a hospital inpatient or emergency room setting and through the use of the facilities and equipment, including qualified electronic health records, of the hospital. The determination of whether an eligible professional is a hospital-based eligible professional shall be made on the basis of the site of service (as defined by the Secretary) and without regard to any employment or billing arrangement between the eligible professional and any other provider.

children.heading: Form of payment
children.label: i Form of payment
children.text: The payment under this paragraph may be in the form of a single consolidated payment or in the form of such periodic installments as the Secretary may specify.

children.heading: Coordination of application of limitation for professionals in different practices
children.label: ii Coordination of application of limitation for professionals in different practices
children.text: In the case of an eligible professional furnishing covered professional services in more than one practice (as specified by the Secretary), the Secretary shall establish rules to coordinate the incentive payments, including the application of the limitation on amounts of such incentive payments under this paragraph, among such practices.

children.heading: Coordination with Medicaid
children.label: iii Coordination with Medicaid
children.text: The Secretary shall seek, to the maximum extent practicable, to avoid duplicative requirements from Federal and State governments to demonstrate meaningful use of certified EHR technology under this subchapter and subchapter XIX. The Secretary may also adjust the reporting periods under such subchapter and such subsections in order to carry out this clause.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “payment year” means a year beginning with 2011.

children.heading: First, second, etc. payment year
children.label: ii First, second, etc. payment year
children.text: The term “first payment year” means, with respect to covered professional services furnished by an eligible professional, the first year for which an incentive payment is made for such services under this subsection. The terms “second payment year”, “third payment year”, “fourth payment year”, and “fifth payment year” mean, with respect to covered professional services furnished by such eligible professional, each successive year immediately following the first payment year for such professional.

children.heading: Meaningful use of certified EHR technology
children.label: i Meaningful use of certified EHR technology
children.text: The eligible professional demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Secretary, in accordance with subparagraph (C)(i), that during such period the professional is using certified EHR technology in a meaningful manner, which shall include the use of electronic prescribing as determined to be appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Information exchange
children.label: ii Information exchange
children.text: The eligible professional demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Secretary, in accordance with subparagraph (C)(i), that during such period such certified EHR technology is connected in a manner that provides, in accordance with law and standards applicable to the exchange of information, for the electronic exchange of health information to improve the quality of health care, such as promoting care coordination, and the professional demonstrates (through a process specified by the Secretary, such as the use of an attestation) that the professional has not knowingly and willfully taken action (such as to disable functionality) to limit or restrict the compatibility or interoperability of the certified EHR technology.

children.heading: Reporting on measures using EHR
children.label: iii Reporting on measures using EHR
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B)(ii) and subsection (q)(5)(B)(ii)(II) and using such certified EHR technology, the eligible professional submits information for such period, in a form and manner specified by the Secretary, on such clinical quality measures and such other measures as selected by the Secretary under subparagraph (B)(i).

children.label: I
children.text: The Secretary shall provide preference to clinical quality measures that have been endorsed by the entity with a contract with the Secretary under .section 1395aaa(a) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: Prior to any measure being selected under this subparagraph, the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register such measure and provide for a period of public comment on such measure.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: The Secretary may not require the electronic reporting of information on clinical quality measures under subparagraph (A)(iii) unless the Secretary has the capacity to accept the information electronically, which may be on a pilot basis.

children.heading: Coordination of reporting of information
children.label: iii Coordination of reporting of information
children.text: In selecting such measures, and in establishing the form and manner for reporting measures under subparagraph (A)(iii), the Secretary shall seek to avoid redundant or duplicative reporting otherwise required, including reporting under subsection (k)(2)(C).

children.heading: Demonstration of meaningful use of certified EHR technology and information exchange
children.label: C Demonstration of meaningful use of certified EHR technology and information exchange
I
children.text: an attestation;

children.label: II
children.text: the submission of claims with appropriate coding (such as a code indicating that a patient encounter was documented using certified EHR technology);

children.label: III
children.text: a survey response;

children.label: IV
children.text: reporting under subparagraph (A)(iii); and

children.label: V
children.text: other means specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Use of part D data
children.label: ii Use of part D data
children.text: Notwithstanding sections 1395w–115(d)(2)(B) and 1395w–115(f)(2) of this title, the Secretary may use data regarding drug claims submitted for purposes of that are necessary for purposes of subparagraph (A).section 1395w–115 of this title

children.heading: Continued application for purposes of MIPS
children.label: D Continued application for purposes of MIPS
children.text: With respect to 2019 and each subsequent payment year, the Secretary shall, for purposes of subsection (q) and in accordance with paragraph (1)(F) of such subsection, determine whether an eligible professional who is a MIPS eligible professional (as defined in subsection (q)(1)(C)) for such year is a meaningful EHR user under this paragraph for the performance period under subsection (q) for such year. The provisions of subparagraphs (B) and (D) of subsection (a)(7), shall apply to assessments of MIPS eligible professionals under subsection (q) with respect to the performance category described in subsection (q)(2)(A)(iv) in an appropriate manner which may be similar to the manner in which such provisions apply with respect to payment adjustments made under subsection (a)(7)(A).5

children.heading: Physician reporting system rules
children.label: A Physician reporting system rules
children.text: Paragraphs (5), (6), and (8) of subsection (k) shall apply for purposes of this subsection in the same manner as they apply for purposes of such subsection.

children.heading: Coordination with other payments
children.label: B Coordination with other payments
children.text: The provisions of this subsection shall not be taken into account in applying the provisions of subsection (m) of this section and of section 1395(m) of this title and any payment under such provisions shall not be taken into account in computing allowable charges under this subsection.l

children.label: i
children.text: the methodology and standards for determining payment amounts under this subsection and payment adjustments under subsection (a)(7)(A), including the limitation under paragraph (1)(B) and coordination under clauses (ii) and (iii) of paragraph (1)(D);

children.label: ii
children.text: the methodology and standards for determining a meaningful EHR user under paragraph (2), including selection of measures under paragraph (2)(B), specification of the means of demonstrating meaningful EHR use under paragraph (2)(C), and the hardship exception under subsection (a)(7)(B);

children.label: iii
children.text: the methodology and standards for determining a hospital-based eligible professional under paragraph (1)(C); and

children.label: iv
children.text: the specification of reporting periods under paragraph (5) and the selection of the form of payment under paragraph (1)(D)(i).

children.heading: Posting on website
children.label: D Posting on website
children.text: The Secretary shall post on the Internet website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, in an easily understandable format, a list of the names, business addresses, and business phone numbers of the eligible professionals who are meaningful EHR users and, as determined appropriate by the Secretary, of group practices receiving incentive payments under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Certified EHR technology defined
children.label: 4 Certified EHR technology defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “certified EHR technology” means a qualified electronic health record (as defined in ) that is certified pursuant to as meeting standards adopted under that are applicable to the type of record involved (as determined by the Secretary, such as an ambulatory electronic health record for office-based physicians or an inpatient hospital electronic health record for hospitals).section 300jj(13) of this titlesection 300jj–11(c)(5) of this titlesection 300jj–14 of this title

children.heading: Covered professional services
children.label: A Covered professional services
children.text: The term “covered professional services” has the meaning given such term in subsection (k)(3).

children.heading: EHR reporting period
children.label: B EHR reporting period
children.text: The term “EHR reporting period” means, with respect to a payment year, any period (or periods) as specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Eligible professional
children.label: C Eligible professional
children.text: The term “eligible professional” means a physician, as defined in .section 1395x(r) of this title

children.heading: Establishment of value-based payment modifier
children.label: p Establishment of value-based payment modifier

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a payment modifier that provides for differential payment to a physician or a group of physicians under the fee schedule established under subsection (b) based upon the quality of care furnished compared to cost (as determined under paragraphs (2) and (3), respectively) during a performance period. Such payment modifier shall be separate from the geographic adjustment factors established under subsection (e).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), quality of care shall be evaluated, to the extent practicable, based on a composite of measures of the quality of care furnished (as established by the Secretary under subparagraph (B)).

children.label: i
children.text: The Secretary shall establish appropriate measures of the quality of care furnished by a physician or group of physicians to individuals enrolled under this part, such as measures that reflect health outcomes. Such measures shall be risk adjusted as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Secretary shall seek endorsement of the measures established under this subparagraph by the entity with a contract under .section 1395aaa(a) of this title

children.heading: Continued application for purposes of MIPS
children.label: C Continued application for purposes of MIPS
children.text: The Secretary shall, in accordance with subsection (q)(1)(F), carry out subparagraph (B) for purposes of subsection (q).

children.heading: Costs
children.label: 3 Costs
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), costs shall be evaluated, to the extent practicable, based on a composite of appropriate measures of costs established by the Secretary (such as the composite measure under the methodology established under subsection (n)(9)(C)(iii)) that eliminate the effect of geographic adjustments in payment rates (as described in subsection (e)), and take into account risk factors (such as socioeconomic and demographic characteristics, ethnicity, and health status of individuals (such as to recognize that less healthy individuals may require more intensive interventions) and other factors determined appropriate by the Secretary. With respect to 2019 and each subsequent year, the Secretary shall, in accordance with subsection (q)(1)(F), carry out this paragraph for purposes of subsection (q).10So in original. Probably should be followed by a second closing parenthesis.10

children.heading: Publication of measures, dates of implementation, performance period
children.label: A Publication of measures, dates of implementation, performance period
i
children.text: The measures of quality of care and costs established under paragraphs (2) and (3), respectively.

children.label: ii
children.text: The dates for implementation of the payment modifier (as determined under subparagraph (B)).

children.label: iii
children.text: The initial performance period (as specified under subparagraph (B)(ii)).

children.heading: Initial implementation
children.label: i Initial implementation
children.text: Subject to the preceding provisions of this subparagraph, the Secretary shall begin implementing the payment modifier established under this subsection through the rulemaking process during 2013 for the physician fee schedule established under subsection (b).

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The Secretary shall specify an initial performance period for application of the payment modifier established under this subsection with respect to 2015.

children.heading: Provision of information during initial performance period
children.label: II Provision of information during initial performance period
children.text: During the initial performance period, the Secretary shall, to the extent practicable, provide information to physicians and groups of physicians about the quality of care furnished by the physician or group of physicians to individuals enrolled under this part compared to cost (as determined under paragraphs (2) and (3), respectively) with respect to the performance period.

children.heading: Application
children.label: iii Application
children.text: The Secretary shall apply the payment modifier established under this subsection for items and services furnished on or after , with respect to specific physicians and groups of physicians the Secretary determines appropriate, and for services furnished on or after , with respect to all physicians and groups of physicians. Such payment modifier shall not be applied for items and services furnished on or after .January 1, 2015January 1, 2017January 1, 2019

children.heading: Budget neutrality
children.label: C Budget neutrality
children.text: The payment modifier established under this subsection shall be implemented in a budget neutral manner.

children.heading: Systems-based care
children.label: 5 Systems-based care
children.text: The Secretary shall, as appropriate, apply the payment modifier established under this subsection in a manner that promotes systems-based care.

children.heading: Consideration of special circumstances of certain providers
children.label: 6 Consideration of special circumstances of certain providers
children.text: In applying the payment modifier under this subsection, the Secretary shall, as appropriate, take into account the special circumstances of physicians or groups of physicians in rural areas and other underserved communities.

children.heading: Application
children.label: 7 Application
children.text: For purposes of the initial application of the payment modifier established under this subsection during the period beginning on , and ending on , the term “physician” has the meaning given such term in . On or after , the Secretary may apply this subsection to eligible professionals (as defined in subsection (k)(3)(B)) as the Secretary determines appropriate.January 1, 2015December 31, 2016January 1, 2017section 1395x(r) of this title

children.heading: Costs
children.label: A Costs
children.text: The term “costs” means expenditures per individual as determined appropriate by the Secretary. In making the determination under the preceding sentence, the Secretary may take into account the amount of growth in expenditures per individual for a physician compared to the amount of such growth for other physicians.

children.heading: Performance period
children.label: B Performance period
children.text: The term “performance period” means a period specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Coordination with other value-based purchasing reforms
children.label: 9 Coordination with other value-based purchasing reforms
children.text: The Secretary shall coordinate the value-based payment modifier established under this subsection with the Physician Feedback Program under subsection (n) and, as the Secretary determines appropriate, other similar provisions of this subchapter.

children.label: A
children.text: the establishment of the value-based payment modifier under this subsection;

children.label: B
children.text: the evaluation of quality of care under paragraph (2), including the establishment of appropriate measures of the quality of care under paragraph (2)(B);

children.label: C
children.text: the evaluation of costs under paragraph (3), including the establishment of appropriate measures of costs under such paragraph;

children.label: D
children.text: the dates for implementation of the value-based payment modifier;

children.label: E
children.text: the specification of the initial performance period and any other performance period under paragraphs (4)(B)(ii) and (8)(B), respectively;

children.label: F
children.text: the application of the value-based payment modifier under paragraph (7); and

children.label: G
children.text: the determination of costs under paragraph (8)(A).

children.label: i
children.text: develop a methodology for assessing the total performance of each MIPS eligible professional according to performance standards under paragraph (3) for a performance period (as established under paragraph (4)) for a year;

children.label: ii
children.text: using such methodology, provide for a composite performance score in accordance with paragraph (5) for each such professional for each performance period; and

children.label: iii
children.text: use such composite performance score of the MIPS eligible professional for a performance period for a year to determine and apply a MIPS adjustment factor (and, as applicable, an additional MIPS adjustment factor) under paragraph (6) to the professional for the year.

children.heading: Program implementation
children.label: B Program implementation
children.text: The MIPS shall apply to payments for covered professional services (as defined in subsection (k)(3)(A)) furnished on or after .January 1, 2019

children.label: I
children.text: for the first and second years for which the MIPS applies to payments (and for the performance period for such first and second year), a physician (as defined in ), a physician assistant, nurse practitioner, and clinical nurse specialist (as such terms are defined in ), a certified registered nurse anesthetist (as defined in ), and a group that includes such professionals; andsection 1395x(r) of this titlesection 1395x(aa)(5) of this titlesection 1395x(bb)(2) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: for the third year for which the MIPS applies to payments (and for the performance period for such third year) and for each succeeding year (and for the performance period for each such year), the professionals described in subclause (I), such other eligible professionals (as defined in subsection (k)(3)(B)) as specified by the Secretary, and a group that includes such professionals.

children.label: I
children.text: is a qualifying APM participant (as defined in section 1395(z)(2) of this title);l

children.label: II
children.text: subject to clause (vii), is a partial qualifying APM participant (as defined in clause (iii)) for the most recent period for which data are available and who, for the performance period with respect to such year, does not report on applicable measures and activities described in paragraph (2)(B) that are required to be reported by such a professional under the MIPS; or

children.label: III
children.text: for the performance period with respect to such year, does not exceed the low-volume threshold measurement selected under clause (iv).

children.label: I
children.text: with respect to 2019 and 2020, the reference in subparagraph (A) of such paragraph to 25 percent was instead a reference to 20 percent;

children.label: aa
children.text: the reference in subparagraph (B)(i) of such paragraph to 50 percent was instead a reference to 40 percent; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the references in subparagraph (B)(ii) of such paragraph to 50 percent and 25 percent of such paragraph were instead references to 40 percent and 20 percent, respectively; and7

children.label: aa
children.text: the reference in subparagraph (C)(i) of such paragraph to 75 percent was instead a reference to 50 percent; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the references in subparagraph (C)(ii) of such paragraph to 75 percent and 25 percent of such paragraph were instead references to 50 percent and 20 percent, respectively.7

children.heading: Selection of low-volume threshold measurement
children.label: iv Selection of low-volume threshold measurement
aa
children.text: for performance periods beginning before , individuals enrolled under this part who are treated by the eligible professional for the performance period involved; andJanuary 1, 2018

children.label: bb
children.text: for performance periods beginning on or after , individuals enrolled under this part who are furnished covered professional services (as defined in subsection (k)(3)(A)) by the eligible professional for the performance period involved.January 1, 2018

children.label: II
children.text: The minimum number (as determined by the Secretary) of covered professional services (as defined in subsection (k)(3)(A)) furnished to individuals enrolled under this part by such professional for such performance period.

children.label: aa
children.text: for performance periods beginning before , allowed charges billed by such professional under this part for such performance period; andJanuary 1, 2018

children.label: bb
children.text: for performance periods beginning on or after , allowed charges for covered professional services (as defined in subsection (k)(3)(A)) billed by such professional for such performance period.January 1, 2018

children.heading: Treatment of new Medicare enrolled eligible professionals
children.label: v Treatment of new Medicare enrolled eligible professionals
children.text: In the case of a professional who first becomes a Medicare enrolled eligible professional during the performance period for a year (and had not previously submitted claims under this subchapter such as a person, an entity, or a part of a physician group or under a different billing number or tax identifier), such professional shall not be treated under this subsection as a MIPS eligible professional until the subsequent year and performance period for such subsequent year.

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: vi Clarification
children.text: In the case of items and services furnished during a year by an individual who is not a MIPS eligible professional (including pursuant to clauses (ii) and (v)) with respect to a year, in no case shall a MIPS adjustment factor (or additional MIPS adjustment factor) under paragraph (6) apply to such individual for such year.

children.heading: Partial qualifying APM participant clarifications
children.label: vii Partial qualifying APM participant clarifications

children.heading: Treatment as MIPS eligible professional
children.label: I Treatment as MIPS eligible professional
children.text: In the case of an eligible professional who is a partial qualifying APM participant, with respect to a year, and who, for the performance period for such year, reports on applicable measures and activities described in paragraph (2)(B) that are required to be reported by such a professional under the MIPS, such eligible professional is considered to be a MIPS eligible professional with respect to such year.

children.heading: Not eligible for qualifying APM participant payments
children.label: II Not eligible for qualifying APM participant payments
children.text: In no case shall an eligible professional who is a partial qualifying APM participant, with respect to a year, be considered a qualifying APM participant (as defined in paragraph (2) of section 1395(z) of this title) for such year or be eligible for the additional payment under paragraph (1) of such section for such year.l

children.heading: Quality performance category
children.label: I Quality performance category
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and apply a process that includes features of the provisions of subsection (m)(3)(C) for MIPS eligible professionals in a group practice with respect to assessing performance of such group with respect to the performance category described in clause (i) of paragraph (2)(A).

children.heading: Other performance categories
children.label: II Other performance categories
children.text: The Secretary may establish and apply a process that includes features of the provisions of subsection (m)(3)(C) for MIPS eligible professionals in a group practice with respect to assessing the performance of such group with respect to the performance categories described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of such paragraph.

children.heading: Ensuring comprehensiveness of group practice assessment
children.label: ii Ensuring comprehensiveness of group practice assessment
children.text: The process established under clause (i) shall to the extent practicable reflect the range of items and services furnished by the MIPS eligible professionals in the group practice involved.

children.heading: Use of registries
children.label: E Use of registries
children.text: Under the MIPS, the Secretary shall encourage the use of qualified clinical data registries pursuant to subsection (m)(3)(E) in carrying out this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: adjust the application of such provision to ensure the provision is consistent with the provisions of this subsection; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not apply such provision to the extent that the provision is duplicative with a provision of this subsection.

children.label: I
children.text: assess appropriate adjustments to quality measures, resource use measures, and other measures used under the MIPS; and

children.label: II
children.text: assess and implement appropriate adjustments to payment adjustments, composite performance scores, scores for performance categories, or scores for measures or activities under the MIPS.

children.heading: Measures and activities under performance categories
children.label: 2 Measures and activities under performance categories
i
children.text: Quality.

children.label: ii
children.text: Resource use.

children.label: iii
children.text: Clinical practice improvement activities.

children.label: iv
children.text: Meaningful use of certified EHR technology.

children.heading: Measures and activities specified for each category
children.label: B Measures and activities specified for each category

children.heading: Quality
children.label: i Quality
children.text: For the performance category described in subparagraph (A)(i), the quality measures included in the final measures list published under subparagraph (D)(i) for such year and the list of quality measures described in subparagraph (D)(vi) used by qualified clinical data registries under subsection (m)(3)(E).

children.heading: Resource use
children.label: ii Resource use
children.text: For the performance category described in subparagraph (A)(ii), the measurement of resource use for such period under subsection (p)(3), using the methodology under subsection (r) as appropriate, and, as feasible and applicable, accounting for the cost of drugs under part D.

children.label: I
children.text: The subcategory of expanded practice access, such as same day appointments for urgent needs and after hours access to clinician advice.

children.label: II
children.text: The subcategory of population management, such as monitoring health conditions of individuals to provide timely health care interventions or participation in a qualified clinical data registry.

children.label: III
children.text: The subcategory of care coordination, such as timely communication of test results, timely exchange of clinical information to patients and other providers, and use of remote monitoring or telehealth.

children.label: IV
children.text: The subcategory of beneficiary engagement, such as the establishment of care plans for individuals with complex care needs, beneficiary self-management assessment and training, and using shared decision-making mechanisms. This subcategory shall include as an activity, for performance periods beginning on or after , use of a real-time benefit tool as described in section 1395w–104() of this title. The Secretary may establish this activity as a standalone or as a component of another activity.January 1, 2022o

children.label: V
children.text: The subcategory of patient safety and practice assessment, such as through use of clinical or surgical checklists and practice assessments related to maintaining certification.

children.label: VI
children.text: The subcategory of participation in an alternative payment model (as defined in section 1395(z)(3)(C) of this title).l

children.heading: Meaningful EHR use
children.label: iv Meaningful EHR use
children.text: For the performance category described in subparagraph (A)(iv), the requirements established for such period under subsection ()(2) for determining whether an eligible professional is a meaningful EHR user.o

children.heading: Emphasizing outcome measures under the quality performance category
children.label: i Emphasizing outcome measures under the quality performance category
children.text: In applying subparagraph (B)(i), the Secretary shall, as feasible, emphasize the application of outcome measures.

children.heading: Application of additional system measures
children.label: ii Application of additional system measures
children.text: The Secretary may use measures used for a payment system other than for physicians, such as measures for inpatient hospitals, for purposes of the performance categories described in clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (A). For purposes of the previous sentence, the Secretary may not use measures for hospital outpatient departments, except in the case of items and services furnished by emergency physicians, radiologists, and anesthesiologists.

children.heading: Global and population-based measures
children.label: iii Global and population-based measures
children.text: The Secretary may use global measures, such as global outcome measures, and population-based measures for purposes of the performance category described in subparagraph (A)(i).

children.heading: Application of measures and activities to non-patient-facing professionals
children.label: iv Application of measures and activities to non-patient-facing professionals
I
children.text: shall give consideration to the circumstances of professional types (or subcategories of those types determined by practice characteristics) who typically furnish services that do not involve face-to-face interaction with a patient; and

children.label: II
children.text: may, to the extent feasible and appropriate, take into account such circumstances and apply under this subsection with respect to MIPS eligible professionals of such professional types or subcategories, alternative measures or activities that fulfill the goals of the applicable performance category.

children.heading: Request for information
children.label: I Request for information
children.text: In initially applying subparagraph (B)(iii), the Secretary shall use a request for information to solicit recommendations from stakeholders to identify activities described in such subparagraph and specifying criteria for such activities.

children.heading: Contract authority for clinical practice improvement activities performance category
children.label: II Contract authority for clinical practice improvement activities performance category
aa
children.text: identifying activities described in subparagraph (B)(iii);

children.label: bb
children.text: specifying criteria for such activities; and

children.label: cc
children.text: determining whether a MIPS eligible professional meets such criteria.

children.heading: Clinical practice improvement activities defined
children.label: III Clinical practice improvement activities defined
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “clinical practice improvement activity” means an activity that relevant eligible professional organizations and other relevant stakeholders identify as improving clinical practice or care delivery and that the Secretary determines, when effectively executed, is likely to result in improved outcomes.

children.heading: Annual list of quality measures available for MIPS assessment
children.label: D Annual list of quality measures available for MIPS assessment
I
children.text: not later than November 1 of the year prior to the first day of the first performance period under the MIPS, establish and publish in the Federal Register a final list of quality measures; and

children.label: aa
children.text: removing from such list, as appropriate, quality measures, which may include the removal of measures that are no longer meaningful (such as measures that are topped out);

children.label: bb
children.text: adding to such list, as appropriate, new quality measures; and

children.label: cc
children.text: determining whether or not quality measures on such list that have undergone substantive changes should be included in the updated list.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Eligible professional organizations and other relevant stakeholders shall be requested to identify and submit quality measures to be considered for selection under this subparagraph in the annual list of quality measures published under clause (i) and to identify and submit updates to the measures on such list. For purposes of the previous sentence, measures may be submitted regardless of whether such measures were previously published in a proposed rule or endorsed by an entity with a contract under .section 1395aaa(a) of this title

children.heading: Eligible professional organization defined
children.label: II Eligible professional organization defined
children.text: In this subparagraph, the term “eligible professional organization” means a professional organization as defined by nationally recognized specialty boards of certification or equivalent certification boards.

children.label: I
children.text: provide that, to the extent practicable, all quality domains (as defined in subsection (s)(1)(B)) are addressed by such measures; and

children.label: II
children.text: ensure that such selection is consistent with the process for selection of measures under subsections (k), (m), and (p)(2).

children.heading: Peer review
children.label: iv Peer review
children.text: Before including a new measure in the final list of measures published under clause (i) for a year, the Secretary shall submit for publication in applicable specialty-appropriate, peer-reviewed journals such measure and the method for developing and selecting such measure, including clinical and other data supporting such measure.

children.label: I
children.text: measures endorsed by a consensus-based entity;

children.label: II
children.text: measures developed under subsection (s); and

children.label: III
children.text: measures submitted under clause (ii)(I).

children.heading: Exception for qualified clinical data registry measures
children.label: vi Exception for qualified clinical data registry measures
children.text: Measures used by a qualified clinical data registry under subsection (m)(3)(E) shall not be subject to the requirements under clauses (i), (iv), and (v). The Secretary shall publish the list of measures used by such qualified clinical data registries on the Internet website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services.

children.heading: Exception for existing quality measures
children.label: vii Exception for existing quality measures
I
children.text: shall not be subject to the requirements under clause (i) (except under items (aa) and (cc) of subclause (II) of such clause) or to the requirement under clause (iv); and

children.label: II
children.text: shall be included in the final list of quality measures published under clause (i) unless removed under clause (i)(II)(aa).

children.heading: Consultation with relevant eligible professional organizations and other relevant stakeholders
children.label: viii Consultation with relevant eligible professional organizations and other relevant stakeholders
children.text: Relevant eligible professional organizations and other relevant stakeholders, including State and national medical societies, shall be consulted in carrying out this subparagraph.

children.heading: Optional application
children.label: ix Optional application
children.text: The process under is not required to apply to the selection of measures under this subparagraph.section 1395aaa–1 of this title

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: Under the MIPS, the Secretary shall establish performance standards with respect to measures and activities specified under paragraph (2)(B) for a performance period (as established under paragraph (4)) for a year.

children.label: i
children.text: Historical performance standards.

children.label: ii
children.text: Improvement.

children.label: iii
children.text: The opportunity for continued improvement.

children.heading: Performance period
children.label: 4 Performance period
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a performance period (or periods) for a year (beginning with 2019). Such performance period (or periods) shall begin and end prior to the beginning of such year and be as close as possible to such year. In this subsection, such performance period (or periods) for a year shall be referred to as the performance period for the year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to the succeeding provisions of this paragraph and taking into account, as available and applicable, paragraph (1)(G), the Secretary shall develop a methodology for assessing the total performance of each MIPS eligible professional according to performance standards under paragraph (3) with respect to applicable measures and activities specified in paragraph (2)(B) with respect to each performance category applicable to such professional for a performance period (as established under paragraph (4)) for a year. Using such methodology, the Secretary shall provide for a composite assessment (using a scoring scale of 0 to 100) for each such professional for the performance period for such year. In this subsection such a composite assessment for such a professional with respect to a performance period shall be referred to as the “composite performance score” for such professional for such performance period.

children.heading: Incentive to report; encouraging use of certified EHR technology for reporting quality measures
children.label: B Incentive to report; encouraging use of certified EHR technology for reporting quality measures

children.heading: Incentive to report
children.label: i Incentive to report
children.text: Under the methodology established under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall provide that in the case of a MIPS eligible professional who fails to report on an applicable measure or activity that is required to be reported by the professional, the professional shall be treated as achieving the lowest potential score applicable to such measure or activity.

children.heading: Encouraging use of certified EHR technology and qualified clinical data registries for reporting quality measures
children.label: ii Encouraging use of certified EHR technology and qualified clinical data registries for reporting quality measures
I
children.text: encourage MIPS eligible professionals to report on applicable measures with respect to the performance category described in paragraph (2)(A)(i) through the use of certified EHR technology and qualified clinical data registries; and

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to a performance period, with respect to a year, for which a MIPS eligible professional reports such measures through the use of such EHR technology, treat such professional as satisfying the clinical quality measures reporting requirement described in subsection ()(2)(A)(iii) for such year.o

children.heading: Clinical practice improvement activities performance score
children.label: C Clinical practice improvement activities performance score

children.heading: Rule for certification
children.label: i Rule for certification
children.text: A MIPS eligible professional who is in a practice that is certified as a patient-centered medical home or comparable specialty practice, as determined by the Secretary, with respect to a performance period shall be given the highest potential score for the performance category described in paragraph (2)(A)(iii) for such period.

children.heading: APM participation
children.label: ii APM participation
children.text: Participation by a MIPS eligible professional in an alternative payment model (as defined in section 1395(z)(3)(C) of this title) with respect to a performance period shall earn such eligible professional a minimum score of one-half of the highest potential score for the performance category described in paragraph (2)(A)(iii) for such performance period.l

children.heading: Subcategories
children.label: iii Subcategories
children.text: A MIPS eligible professional shall not be required to perform activities in each subcategory under paragraph (2)(B)(iii) or participate in an alternative payment model in order to achieve the highest potential score for the performance category described in paragraph (2)(A)(iii).

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of the performance score for the performance category described in clauses (i) and (ii) of paragraph (2)(A), subject to clause (iii), shall take into account the improvement of the professional; and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of performance scores for other performance categories, may take into account the improvement of the professional.

children.heading: Assigning higher weight for achievement
children.label: ii Assigning higher weight for achievement
children.text: Subject to clause (i), under the methodology developed under subparagraph (A), the Secretary may assign a higher scoring weight under subparagraph (F) with respect to the achievement of a MIPS eligible professional than with respect to any improvement of such professional applied under clause (i) with respect to a measure, activity, or category described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Transition years
children.label: iii Transition years
children.text: For each of the second, third, fourth, and fifth years for which the MIPS applies to payments, the performance score for the performance category described in paragraph (2)(A)(ii) shall not take into account the improvement of the professional involved.

children.heading: Weights for the performance categories
children.label: E Weights for the performance categories

children.heading: In general
children.label: aa In general
children.text: Subject to item (bb), thirty percent of such score shall be based on performance with respect to the category described in clause (i) of paragraph (2)(A). In applying the previous sentence, the Secretary shall, as feasible, encourage the application of outcome measures within such category.

children.heading: First 5 years
children.label: bb First 5 years
children.text: For each of the first through fifth years for which the MIPS applies to payments, the percentage applicable under item (aa) shall be increased in a manner such that the total percentage points of the increase under this item for the respective year equals the total number of percentage points by which the percentage applied under subclause (II)(bb) for the respective year is less than 30 percent.

children.heading: In general
children.label: aa In general
children.text: Subject to item (bb), thirty percent of such score shall be based on performance with respect to the category described in clause (ii) of paragraph (2)(A).

children.heading: First 5 years
children.label: bb First 5 years
children.text: For the first year for which the MIPS applies to payments, not more than 10 percent of such score shall be based on performance with respect to the category described in clause (ii) of paragraph (2)(A). For each of the second, third, fourth, and fifth years for which the MIPS applies to payments, not less than 10 percent and not more than 30 percent of such score shall be based on performance with respect to the category described in clause (ii) of paragraph (2)(A). Nothing in the previous sentence shall be construed, with respect to a performance period for a year described in the previous sentence, as preventing the Secretary from basing 30 percent of such score for such year with respect to the category described in such clause (ii), if the Secretary determines, based on information posted under subsection (r)(2)(I) that sufficient resource use measures are ready for adoption for use under the performance category under paragraph (2)(A)(ii) for such performance period.

children.heading: Clinical practice improvement activities
children.label: III Clinical practice improvement activities
children.text: Fifteen percent of such score shall be based on performance with respect to the category described in clause (iii) of paragraph (2)(A).

children.heading: Meaningful use of certified EHR technology
children.label: IV Meaningful use of certified EHR technology
children.text: Twenty-five percent of such score shall be based on performance with respect to the category described in clause (iv) of paragraph (2)(A).

children.heading: Authority to adjust percentages in case of high EHR meaningful use adoption
children.label: ii Authority to adjust percentages in case of high EHR meaningful use adoption
children.text: In any year in which the Secretary estimates that the proportion of eligible professionals (as defined in subsection ()(5)) who are meaningful EHR users (as determined under subsection ()(2)) is 75 percent or greater, the Secretary may reduce the percent applicable under clause (i)(IV), but not below 15 percent. If the Secretary makes such reduction for a year, subject to subclauses (I)(bb) and (II)(bb) of clause (i), the percentages applicable under one or more of subclauses (I), (II), and (III) of clause (i) for such year shall be increased in a manner such that the total percentage points of the increase under this clause for such year equals the total number of percentage points reduced under the preceding sentence for such year.oo

children.heading: Certain flexibility for weighting performance categories, measures, and activities
children.label: F Certain flexibility for weighting performance categories, measures, and activities
i
children.text: which may vary from the scoring weights specified in subparagraph (E), for each performance category based on the extent to which the category is applicable to the type of eligible professional involved; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for each measure and activity specified under paragraph (2)(B) with respect to each such category based on the extent to which the measure or activity is applicable and available to the type of eligible professional involved.

children.heading: Resource use
children.label: G Resource use
children.text: Analysis of the performance category described in paragraph (2)(A)(ii) shall include results from the methodology described in subsection (r)(5), as appropriate.

children.heading: Inclusion of quality measure data from other payers
children.label: H Inclusion of quality measure data from other payers
children.text: In applying subsections (k), (m), and (p) with respect to measures described in paragraph (2)(B)(i), analysis of the performance category described in paragraph (2)(A)(i) may include data submitted by MIPS eligible professionals with respect to items and services furnished to individuals who are not individuals entitled to benefits under part A or enrolled under part B.

children.heading: Use of voluntary virtual groups for certain assessment purposes
children.label: I Use of voluntary virtual groups for certain assessment purposes
I
children.text: the assessment of performance provided under such methodology with respect to such performance categories that is to be applied to each such professional in such group for such performance period shall be with respect to the combined performance of all such professionals in such group for such period; and

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to the composite performance score provided under this paragraph for such performance period for each such MIPS eligible professional in such virtual group, the components of the composite performance score that assess performance with respect to such performance categories shall be based on the assessment of the combined performance under subclause (I) for such performance categories and performance period.

children.heading: Election of practices to be a virtual group
children.label: ii Election of practices to be a virtual group
children.text: The Secretary shall, in accordance with the requirements under clause (iii), establish and have in place a process to allow an individual MIPS eligible professional or a group practice consisting of not more than 10 MIPS eligible professionals to elect, with respect to a performance period for a year to be a virtual group under this subparagraph with at least one other such individual MIPS eligible professional or group practice. Such a virtual group may be based on appropriate classifications of providers, such as by geographic areas or by provider specialties defined by nationally recognized specialty boards of certification or equivalent certification boards.

children.label: I
children.text: provide that an election under such clause, with respect to a performance period, shall be made before the beginning of such performance period and may not be changed during such performance period;

children.label: II
children.text: provide that an individual MIPS eligible professional and a group practice described in clause (ii) may elect to be in no more than one virtual group for a performance period and that, in the case of such a group practice that elects to be in such virtual group for such performance period, such election applies to all MIPS eligible professionals in such group practice;

children.label: III
children.text: provide that a virtual group be a combination of tax identification numbers;

children.label: IV
children.text: provide for formal written agreements among MIPS eligible professionals electing to be a virtual group under this subparagraph; and

children.label: V
children.text: include such other requirements as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: by comparing the composite performance score of the eligible professional for such year to the performance threshold established under subparagraph (D)(i) for such year;

children.label: I
children.text: MIPS eligible professionals with composite performance scores for such year at or above such performance threshold for such year receive zero or positive payment adjustment factors for such year in accordance with clause (iii), with such professionals having higher composite performance scores receiving higher adjustment factors; and

children.label: II
children.text: MIPS eligible professionals with composite performance scores for such year below such performance threshold for such year receive negative payment adjustment factors for such year in accordance with clause (iv), with such professionals having lower composite performance scores receiving lower adjustment factors;

children.label: iii
children.text: in a manner such that MIPS eligible professionals with composite scores described in clause (ii)(I) for such year, subject to clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (F), receive a zero or positive adjustment factor on a linear sliding scale such that an adjustment factor of 0 percent is assigned for a score at the performance threshold and an adjustment factor of the applicable percent specified in subparagraph (B) is assigned for a score of 100; and

children.label: I
children.text: subject to subclause (II), MIPS eligible professionals with composite performance scores described in clause (ii)(II) for such year receive a negative payment adjustment factor on a linear sliding scale such that an adjustment factor of 0 percent is assigned for a score at the performance threshold and an adjustment factor of the negative of the applicable percent specified in subparagraph (B) is assigned for a score of 0; and

children.label: II
children.text: MIPS eligible professionals with composite performance scores that are equal to or greater than 0, but not greater than ¼ of the performance threshold specified under subparagraph (D)(i) for such year, receive a negative payment adjustment factor that is equal to the negative of the applicable percent specified in subparagraph (B) for such year.

children.label: i
children.text: for 2019, 4 percent;

children.label: ii
children.text: for 2020, 5 percent;

children.label: iii
children.text: for 2021, 7 percent; and

children.label: iv
children.text: for 2022 and subsequent years, 9 percent.

children.heading: Additional MIPS adjustment factors for exceptional performance
children.label: C Additional MIPS adjustment factors for exceptional performance
children.text: For 2019 and each subsequent year through 2024, in the case of a MIPS eligible professional with a composite performance score for a year at or above the additional performance threshold under subparagraph (D)(ii) for such year, in addition to the MIPS adjustment factor under subparagraph (A) for the eligible professional for such year, subject to subparagraph (F)(iv), the Secretary shall specify an additional positive MIPS adjustment factor for such professional and year.
